## 大( )同( )区(區)
---
## Datong District, Taipei
### Dàtóng Qū
---
- **traditional:** 大同區
- **definition:** Datong District, Taipei
- **pinyin:** Dàtóng Qū
- **components:**
  - 大同
    - dàtóng
    - Great Unity; place name
  - 区(區)
    - qū
    - area; district
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 大 (dà, "big; great") conveys size or importance, 同 (tóng, "same; together") suggests unity or harmony, and 區 (qū, "area; district") designates a geographical division.
  - **interpretation:** 大同 (dàtóng, "Great Unity") originates from a Confucian ideal in the Book of Rites (禮記/礼记), describing a utopian society characterized by harmony and fairness; it refers here to a place name, conveying a sense of significant unity or community. 區 (qū, "area; district") simply denotes the administrative division.
- **examples:**
  - 大( )同( )区(區)有( )许(許)多( )历(歷)史( )建( )筑(築)。
    - Dàtóng Qū yǒu xǔduō lìshǐ jiànzhù.
    - Datong District has many historical buildings.
  - 我( )想( )去( )大( )同( )区(區)的夜市。
    - Wǒ xiǎng qù Dàtóng Qū de yèshì.
    - I want to visit the night market in Datong District.
  - 大( )同( )区(區)是( )台( )北( )的( )重( )要( )区(區)域( )。
    - Dàtóng Qū shì Táiběi de zhòngyào qūyù.
    - Datong District is an important area in Taipei.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同
- **definition:** Great Unity; place name
- **pinyin:** dàtóng
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big; great
  - 同
    - tóng
    - same; together
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 大 (dà, "big; great") + 同 (tóng, "same; together") =
    - Great Unity
  - **interpretation:** 大 (dà, "big; great") signifies something vast or significant. 同 (tóng, "same; together") indicates a sense of unity or coming together. In the Confucian context, 大同 (dàtóng, "Great Unity") refers to an ideal world where harmony and shared interests prevail, a vision detailed in the Book of Rites (禮記/礼记), aiming for a society where everyone works towards common welfare. The concept involves worldly unity and equality, projecting a utopian social order. Additionally, 大同 (dàtóng, "Datong") is a place name, reflecting either the historical influence or aspiration for such a harmony in certain regions.
- **examples:**
  - 大( )同( )是( )一( )个(個)历(歷)史( )悠( )久( )的( )城( )市( )。
    - Dàtóng shì yí gè lìshǐ yōujiǔ de chéngshì.
    - Datong is a city with a long history.
  - 理( )想( )的( )社( )会(會)应(應)该(該)实(實)现(現)大( )同( )。
    - Lǐxiǎng de shèhuì yīnggāi shíxiàn dàtóng.
    - An ideal society should achieve Great Unity.
  - 他( )计(計)划(劃)今( )年( )去( )大( )同( )旅( )游(遊)。
    - Tā jìhuà jīnnián qù Dàtóng lǚyóu.
    - He plans to travel to Datong this year.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 大
- **definition:** big; great
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 大](@media/glyph5927.png)
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 大 → 夫
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 夫](@media/glyph592b.png)
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 同
- **definition:** same; together
- **pinyin:** tóng
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - "mouth"
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally depicted as a pipe 筒 (tǒng, "pipe") ->
    - 口 added as decoration ->
    - interpreted as mouth 口 (kǒu, "mouth") giving orders ->
    - evolved to mean togetherness and unity
  - **interpretation:** The character 同 (tóng, "same; unity; together; common") originally depicted a pipe but later took on the symbolic meaning of unity, possibly influenced by the shared cooperation in activities like music or rigid group tasks. The addition of 口 (kǒu, "mouth") emphasizes communication and collaboration.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)同( )心( )协(協)力( )。
    - Wǒmen tóngxīn xiélì.
    - We work together with one heart and mind.
  - 他( )们(們)穿( )着(著)同( )款( )衣( )服( )，看( )起( )来(來)十( )分( )整( )齐(齊)。
    - Tāmen chuānzhe tóng kuǎn yīfú, kàn qǐlái shífēn zhěngqí.
    - They are wearing the same style of clothes and look very orderly.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 同](@media/glyph540c.png)
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 同 → 口
- **definition:** "mouth"
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 口](@media/glyph53e3.png)
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區)
- **traditional:** 區
- **definition:** area; district
- **pinyin:** qū
- **components:**
  - 匸
    - xì
    - enclosure
  - 品
    - pǐn
    - goods; quality; many people
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 匸 (a container or enclosure) + 品 (many people) =
    - people inside an area ->
    - district; region; area
  - **interpretation:** The character represents many people (品) within a boundary or enclosure (匸), intuitively suggesting a designated area or district where people congregate and interact.
  - **simplification:** 區 (qū, "district; region; area") was simplified by replacing 品 (pǐn, "goods") with 乂 (yì, "to govern"), reducing the complexity while retaining the structure of 匸 (xì, "enclosure").
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)区(區)域( )的( )空( )气(氣)非( )常( )清( )新( )。
    - Zhège qūyù de kōngqì fēicháng qīngxīn.
    - The air in this area is very fresh.
  - 我( )们(們)住( )在( )城( )市( )的( )商( )业(業)区(區)。
    - Wǒmen zhù zài chéngshì de shāngyèqū.
    - We live in the commercial district of the city.
  - 该(該)地( )区(區)以( )其( )风(風)景( )优(優)美( )而( )闻(聞)名( )。
    - Gāi dìqū yǐ qí fēngjǐng yōuměi ér wénmíng.
    - The region is known for its beautiful scenery.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區) → 匸
- **definition:** enclosure
- **pinyin:** xì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 𠃊 (symbolic graphic) ->
    - depicts an enclosure hiding something or someone ->
    - evolved to represent a concealed space or item
  - **interpretation:** The character 匸(xì, 'box') is a pictogram representing a concealed or enclosed space. Its graphical form suggests something contained or hidden, which aligns with its meaning as a 'box' or an enclosure. Unlike other related pictograms that depict physical containers, 匸 emphasizes the aspect of concealment or secrecy.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )把( )匸( )搬( )到( )了( )房( )间(間)角( )落( )。
    - Tā bǎ xì bān dào le fángjiān jiǎoluò.
    - He moved the box to the corner of the room.
  - 我( )们(們)在( )仓(倉)库(庫)找( )到( )了( )匸( )，里(裡)面( )装(裝)有( )秘( )密( )文( )件( )。
    - Wǒmen zài cāngkù zhǎodào le xì, lǐmiàn zhuāng yǒu mìmì wénjiàn.
    - We found the box in the warehouse, and it contained secret documents.
  - 工( )厂(廠)工( )人( )把( )旧(舊)匸( )清( )理( )了( )，然( )后(後)再( )利( )用( )。
    - Gōngchǎng gōngrén bǎ jiù xì qīnglǐ le, ránhòu zài lìyòng.
    - Factory workers cleaned the old box and then reused it.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 匸](@media/glyph5338.png)
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區) → 品
- **definition:** goods; quality; many people
- **pinyin:** pǐn
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Three 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') arranged together =
    - multiple mouths speaking or consuming ->
    - representing many or various items
  - **interpretation:** The use of three mouths suggests plurality or variety. It's similar in structure to 晶 (jīng, 'sparkle'), which uses three 日 (rì, 'sun') to suggest brightness or vibrancy with multiple light sources.
- **examples:**
  - 比( )赛(賽)吸( )引( )了( )数(數)以( )千( )计(計)的( )品( )，个(個)个(個)英( )姿( )飒(颯)爽( )。
    - Bǐsài xīyǐnle shù yǐ qiān jì de pǐn, gège yīngzī sàshuǎng.
    - The competition attracted thousands of people, and everyone looked heroic.
  - 节(節)日( )庆(慶)典( )上( )品( )众(眾)多( )，场(場)面( )热(熱)烈( )非( )凡( )。
    - Jiérì qìngdiǎn shàng pǐn zhòngduō, chǎngmiàn rèliè fēifán.
    - The festive celebration saw a large crowd, and the scene was extraordinarily lively.
  - 广(廣)场(場)上( )聚( )集( )了( )众(眾)多( )品( )，大( )家( )热(熱)情( )互( )动(動)。
    - Guǎngchǎng shàng jùjíle zhòngduō pǐn, dàjiā rèqíng hùdòng.
    - The square gathered a multitude of people, and everyone interacted enthusiastically.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 品](@media/glyph54c1.png)

## 三弦
---
## three-stringed Chinese lute used in Pingtan
### sānxián
---
- **definition:** three-stringed Chinese lute used in Pingtan
- **pinyin:** sānxián
- **components:**
  - 三
    - sān
    - three
  - 弦
    - xián
    - string; chord
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 三 (sān, "three"), 弦 (xián, "string")
  - **interpretation:** The word 三弦 (sānxián) literally describes a musical instrument with 'three strings'. The numeral 三 (sān, "three") specifies the number of strings on the instrument, while 弦 (xián, "string; chord") denotes the part of the lute that produces sound. This particular lute, used in traditional Chinese music like Pingtan, emphasizes the design feature of having exactly three strings, distinguishing it from other string instruments.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )正( )在( )学(學)习(習)演( )奏( )三( )弦( )。
    - Tā zhèngzài xuéxí yǎnzòu sānxián.
    - He is learning to play the three-stringed lute.
  - 三( )弦( )的( )声(聲)音( )优(優)美( )动(動)人( )。
    - Sānxián de shēngyīn yōuměi dòngrén.
    - The sound of the three-stringed lute is beautiful and moving.
  - 在( )苏(蘇)州( )，他( )们(們)常( )用( )三( )弦( )演( )奏( )评(評)弹(彈)。
    - Zài Sūzhōu, tāmen cháng yòng sānxián yǎnzòu píngtán.
    - In Suzhou, they often use the three-stringed lute to perform Pingtan.
### 三弦 → 三
- **definition:** three
- **pinyin:** sān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 一 (yī, "one") triplicated ->
    - 三 (sān, "three")
  - **interpretation:** The character 三 (sān, "three") is an ideogram depicting the concept of the number three through the use of three parallel horizontal strokes. This straightforward representation makes it consistent with related numeric characters like 一 (yī, "one") and 二 (èr, "two").
- **examples:**
  - 今( )天( )我( )买(買)了( )三( )斤( )苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Jīntiān wǒ mǎi le sān jīn píngguǒ.
    - Today I bought three jin of apples.
  - 他( )有( )三( )支( )笔(筆)，我只想要其中一支。
    - Tā yǒu sān zhī bǐ, wǒ zhǐ xiǎng yào qízhōng yī zhī.
    - He has three pens, but I only want one of them.
  - 他( )们(們)吃( )了( )三( )碗( )面(麵)，觉(覺)得( )非( )常( )满(滿)意( )。
    - Tāmen chī le sān wǎn miàn, juéde fēicháng mǎnyì.
    - They ate three bowls of noodles and felt very satisfied.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 三](@media/glyph4e09.png)
### 三弦 → 弦(絃)
- **traditional:** 絃
- **definition:** string; chord
- **pinyin:** xián
- **components:**
  - 糸
    - sī
    - silk; thread
  - 玄
    - xuán
    - dark; profound
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 糸 (sī, "silk; thread") + phonetic 玄 (xuán) ->
    - string
  - **interpretation:** The character 絃 (xián, "string") combines the semantic component 糸 (sī, "silk; thread"), which indicates its association with threads or strings, with the phonetic component 玄 (xuán) to provide the sound. The use of 'silk' (糸) reflects the traditional material for making strings in musical instruments, directly linking to its meaning 'string'.
  - **simplification:** The traditional character 絃 was simplified to 弦 by replacing 糸 (sī, "silk; thread") with 弓 (gōng, "bow"). This changes the initial component to one less related to the material but still retains the phonetic 玄 (xuán).
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )吉( )他( )上( )缺( )了( )一( )根( )弦(絃)。
    - Tā de jítā shàng quēle yī gēn xián.
    - His guitar is missing a string.
  - 弹(彈)琴( )时(時)他( )总(總)是( )调(調)松(鬆)一( )根( )弦(絃)。
    - Tán qín shí tā zǒng shì tiáo sōng yī gēn xián.
    - He always loosens a string when playing the piano.
  - 这(這)些( )乐(樂)器( )的( )弦(絃)非( )常( )精( )致(緻)。
    - Zhèxiē yuèqì de xián fēicháng jīngzhì.
    - The strings of these instruments are very delicate.
### 三弦 → 弦(絃) → 糸
- **definition:** silk; thread
- **pinyin:** sī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a skein of silk
  - **interpretation:** The character 糸(mì, "silk; thread") represents a pictorial representation of a skein of silk, visually capturing the concept of thread or fiber.
- **examples:**
  - 她( )手( )工( )制(製)作( )了( )一( )条(條)漂( )亮( )的( )丝(絲)巾( )。
    - Tā shǒugōng zhìzuòle yī tiáo piàoliang de sī jīn.
    - She handcrafted a beautiful silk scarf.
  - 这(這)个(個)设(設)计(計)图(圖)必( )须(須)用( )细(細)糸( )画(畫)出( )来(來)。
    - Zhège shèjìtú bìxū yòng xì mì huà chūlái.
    - This design drawing must be sketched with fine thread.
  - 我( )们(們)参(參)观(觀)了( )古( )代( )的( )织(織)布( )技( )术(術)，对(對)丝(絲)绸(綢)制(製)作( )产(產)生( )了( )兴(興)趣( )。
    - Wǒmen cānguānle gǔdài de zhībù jìshù, duì sīchóu zhìzuò chǎnshēngle xìngqù.
    - We visited ancient weaving techniques and developed an interest in silk production.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 糸](@media/glyph7cf8.png)
### 三弦 → 弦(絃) → 玄
- **definition:** dark; profound
- **pinyin:** xuán
- **components:**
  - 幺
    - yāo
    - tiny threads
  - 亠
    - tóu
    - top mark
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 幺 (yāo, "tiny threads") + 亠 (tóu, "top mark") =
    - two interwoven threads with a top mark representing a rope ->
    - dark/mysterious
  - **interpretation:** The character combines tiny threads 幺 with a distinguishing top mark 亠, symbolizing complexity or intricacy akin to a rope. This imagery connects to its meaning of something dark or mysterious, possibly alluding to complexity not easily understood.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，大( )地( )笼(籠)罩( )着(著)玄( )秘( )的( )色( )彩( )。
    - Yèmù jiànglín shí, dàdì lǒngzhào zhe xuánmì de sècǎi.
    - When night falls, the earth is enveloped by mysterious, dark hues.
  - 这(這)幅( )山( )水( )画(畫)充( )满(滿)玄( )妙( )意( )境( )。
    - Zhè fú shānshuǐhuà chōngmǎn xuánmiào yìjìng.
    - This landscape painting is full of mystical allure.
  - 他的故事令( )人( )感( )到( )玄( )虚(虛)莫( )测(測)。
    - Tā de gùshì lìngrén gǎndào xuánxū mòcè.
    - His story gives an impression of enigmatic inscrutability.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 玄](@media/glyph7384.png)
### 三弦 → 弦(絃) → 玄 → 幺
- **definition:** tiny threads
- **pinyin:** yāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a thin silk thread
  - **interpretation:** The character 幺 (yāo, "tiny threads") illustrates a thin silk thread, reflecting its meaning directly through its shape. It resembles a representation of something small and delicate, like the strands of silk used in weaving.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)微( )小( )的( )幺( )在( )织(織)物( )中( )闪(閃)耀( )。
    - Zhège wéixiǎo de yāo zài zhīwù zhōng shǎnyào.
    - The tiny thread sparkles within the fabric.
  - 织(織)布( )工( )巧( )妙( )地( )利( )用( )幺( )，将(將)其( )编(編)织(織)进(進)精( )美( )的( )锦(錦)缎(緞)中( )。
    - Zhībùgōng qiǎomiào de lìyòng yāo, jiāng qí biānzhī jìn jīngměi de jǐnduàn zhōng.
    - The weaver cleverly employs the tiny thread, integrating it into exquisite brocade.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 幺](@media/glyph5e7a.png)
### 三弦 → 弦(絃) → 玄 → 亠
- **definition:** top mark
- **pinyin:** tóu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Couvercle with a handle ->
    - false friend: roof of a tower in 高 (gāo, "high") ->
    - head and open arms in 立 (lì, "stand")/交 (jiāo, "exchange") ->
    - roof of a hut in 六 (liù, "six") ->
    - top of traditional cloth in 衣 (yī, "clothing") ->
    - mark over silk threads in 玄 (xuán, "mysterious") ->
    - top of knife/chisel to mark prisoners in 辛 (xīn, "bitter") ->
    - grass in 离 (lí, "leave")
  - **interpretation:** 亠 is primarily a pictogram that initially represented a cover with a handle. However, its usage varies in different characters, symbolizing roofs, heads, tops of clothing, and marks, showing its flexibility and evolution in form and meaning across contexts.
- **examples:**
  - 在( )汉(漢)字( )结(結)构(構)中( )，亠( )作( )为(為)顶(頂)标(標)发(發)挥(揮)作( )用( )。
    - Zài hànzì jiégòu zhōng, tóu zuòwéi dǐngbiāo fāhuī zuòyòng.
    - In the structure of Chinese characters, 亠 acts as a top mark to exert its function.
  - 古( )代( )书(書)法( )作( )品( )中( )，亠( )显(顯)示( )出( )独(獨)特( )的( )审(審)美( )价(價)值( )。
    - Gǔdài shūfǎ zuòpǐn zhōng, tóu xiǎnshì chū dútè de shěnměi jiàzhí.
    - In ancient calligraphy, 亠 demonstrates a unique aesthetic value.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 亠](@media/glyph4ea0.png)

## 庆(慶)
---
## celebrate
### qìng
---
- **traditional:** 慶
- **definition:** celebrate
- **pinyin:** qìng
- **components:**
  - 廌
    - zhì
    - antelope
  - 心
    - xīn
    - heart
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 廌 (antelope) + 心 (heart) =
    - auspiciousness ->
    - celebration
  - **interpretation:** The character 慶 (qìng, 'celebrate') originally combines 廌 (zhì, 'antelope') and 心 (xīn, 'heart'). The antelope, due to its beauty, symbolizes something worth celebrating or a blessing when obtained, while the heart represents emotions like goodness. This combination conveys the idea of celebration through the joy and positivity associated with possessing something precious.
  - **simplification:** The traditional character 慶 was simplified to 庆 by eliminating the bottom right component and replacing it with 大 (dà, 'big').
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)将(將)在( )周(週)末( )庆(慶)祝( )你( )的( )生( )日( )。
    - Wǒmen jiāng zài zhōumò qìngzhù nǐ de shēngrì.
    - We will celebrate your birthday on the weekend.
  - 每( )年( )国(國)庆(慶)节(節)我( )们(們)都( )会(會)放( )假( )。
    - Měinián guóqìng jié wǒmen dōu huì fàngjià.
    - We have a holiday every National Day.
  - 他( )们(們)为(為)新( )年( )的( )到( )来(來)举(舉)办(辦)了( )盛( )大( )的( )庆(慶)典( )。
    - Tāmen wèi xīnnián de dàolái jǔbànle shèngdà de qìngdiǎn.
    - They held a grand celebration for the arrival of the New Year.
### 庆(慶) → 廌
- **definition:** antelope
- **pinyin:** zhì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a deer-like mythical beast
  - **interpretation:** The character 廌 (zhì, "antelope") depicts a mythical creature resembling a deer, which symbolizes this ancient representation of antelope.
### 庆(慶) → 心
- **definition:** heart
- **pinyin:** xīn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a heart
  - **interpretation:** The character 心(xīn, "heart") originally depicted the shape of an actual heart, representing thoughts, emotions, and the core of a person. Over time, it has become highly stylized, but it continues to convey the notion of the heart as the center of emotions and consciousness.
- **examples:**
  - 她的心一( )直( )都( )在( )帮(幫)助( )别(別)人( )。
    - Tā de xīn yīzhí dōu zài bāngzhù biérén.
    - Her heart is always in helping others.
  - 在城市的中心，有( )一( )个(個)美( )丽(麗)的( )公( )园(園)。
    - Zài chéngshì de zhōngxīn, yǒu yí gè měilì de gōngyuán.
    - At the center of the city, there is a beautiful park.
  - 对(對)于(於)许(許)多( )人( )来(來)说(說)，家庭是生活的核心。
    - Duìyú xǔduō rén lái shuō, jiātíng shì shēnghuó de héxīn.
    - For many people, family is the core of life.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 心](@media/glyph5fc3.png)

## 凉(涼)茶( )
---
## herbal tea
### liángchá
---
- **traditional:** 涼茶
- **definition:** herbal tea
- **pinyin:** liángchá
- **components:**
  - 凉(涼)
    - liáng
    - cool
  - 茶
    - chá
    - tea
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 涼(凉) (liáng, "cool") + 茶 (chá, "tea") =
    - herbal tea
  - **interpretation:** 涼茶 (liángchá, "herbal tea") refers to a type of tea believed to have cooling properties, which helps reduce internal heat according to traditional Chinese medicinal beliefs. The combination makes sense as 涼(凉) (liáng, "cool") implies the cooling effect the tea is supposed to provide.
- **examples:**
  - 夏( )天( )很( )热(熱)，喝( )一( )杯( )凉(涼)茶( )。
    - Xiàtiān hěn rè, hē yī bēi liángchá.
    - It's very hot in summer, so drink a cup of herbal tea.
  - 凉(涼)茶( )解( )渴( )。
    - Liángchá jiě kě.
    - Herbal tea quenches thirst.
  - 忙( )碌( )一( )天( )后(後)，喝( )点(點)凉(涼)茶( )。
    - Mánglù yī tiān hòu, hē diǎn liángchá.
    - After a busy day, have some herbal tea.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 凉(涼)
- **traditional:** 涼
- **definition:** cool
- **pinyin:** liáng
- **components:**
  - 水
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 京
    - jīng
    - capital (city); used phonetically
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 京 (jīng) ->
    - cool temperature
  - **interpretation:** The character 涼 (liáng, "cool") combines the semantic component 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water"), representing the cooling effect of water, with the phonetic component 京 (jīng) for its sound. This combination intuitively suggests a sense of coolness, as water often has a cooling effect, while 京 provides the sound for the word.
  - **simplification:** 凉 is a simplified form where the radical 氵was replaced with 冫(bīng, "ice") to keep the reference to coldness and further reduce stroke count.
- **examples:**
  - 风(風)吹( )来(來)的( )时(時)候( )很( )凉(涼)。
    - Fēng chuī lái de shíhòu hěn liáng.
    - It's cool when the wind blows.
  - 凉(涼)爽( )的( )夜( )晚( )让(讓)人( )感( )到( )舒( )适(適)。
    - Liángshuǎng de yèwǎn ràng rén gǎndào shūshì.
    - A cool night feels comfortable.
  - 这(這)杯( )茶( )已( )经(經)凉(涼)了( )。
    - Zhè bēi chá yǐjīng liáng le.
    - This cup of tea has already cooled down.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 凉(涼) → 水
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts flowing water
  - **interpretation:** 水 (shuǐ, 'water') is a pictogram that directly represents the appearance of flowing water, making it intuitive and easy to recognize. Its simplicity and direct visual reference to its meaning have allowed it to remain unchanged over millennia.
- **examples:**
  - 水( )是( )一( )种(種)生( )命( )之( )源( )。
    - Shuǐ shì yī zhǒng shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 夏( )天( )很( )热(熱)，喝水能解暑。
    - Xiàtiān hěn rè, hē shuǐ néng jiě shǔ.
    - It is very hot in summer, drinking water can relieve the heat.
  - 河水潺潺，沿( )岸( )绿(綠)树(樹)成( )荫(蔭)。
    - Hé shuǐ chánchán, yán'àn lǜshù chéng yīn.
    - The river flows babbling, and green trees along the bank provide shade.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 水](@media/glyph6c34.png)
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 凉(涼) → 京
- **definition:** capital (city); used phonetically
- **pinyin:** jīng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tall building or tower
  - **interpretation:** The character 京 (jīng, "tower") is a pictogram representing a tall building or a tower. Its form directly illustrates the concept of height and grandeur associated with a prominent structure.
- **examples:**
  - 故( )宫(宮)的( )塔( )楼(樓)很( )高( )。
    - Gùgōng de tǎlóu hěn gāo.
    - The tower of the Forbidden City is very tall.
  - 在北京，你( )可( )以( )看( )到( )许(許)多( )高( )楼(樓)大( )厦(廈)。
    - Zài Běijīng, nǐ kěyǐ kàn dào xǔduō gāolóu dàshà.
    - In Beijing, you can see many tall buildings.
  - 古( )代( )的( )建( )筑(築)中( )常( )常( )能( )见(見)到( )高( )耸(聳)的( )塔( )顶(頂)。
    - Gǔdài de jiànzhú zhōng chángcháng néng jiàn dào gāosǒng de tǎdǐng.
    - In ancient architecture, towering spires can often be seen.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 京](@media/glyph4eac.png)
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶
- **definition:** tea
- **pinyin:** chá
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass
  - 余
    - yú
    - I; surplus
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 艹 (cǎo, "grass") + phonetic 余 (yú) ->
    - modified from archaic 荼 (bitter plant) for tea
  - **interpretation:** The character 茶 (chá, "tea") combines 艹 (cǎo, "grass") as the semantic element, indicating a plant-related concept, with 余 (yú) serving as the phonetic component. Originally, 茶 was a modification of the archaic character 荼 which referred to a 'bitter plant.' This association with a bitter plant reflects the historical use of tea leaves, which have a bitter taste, thereby making 茶 appropriate for representing 'tea.'
- **examples:**
  - 奶奶每天早上喝茶。
    - Nǎinai měitiān zǎoshang hē chá.
    - Grandma drinks tea every morning.
  - 这(這)家( )茶( )馆(館)的( )绿(綠)茶( )味( )道( )很( )好( )。
    - Zhè jiā cháguǎn de lǜchá wèidào hěn hǎo.
    - The green tea at this teahouse tastes very good.
  - 你( )想( )喝( )红(紅)茶( )还(還)是( )绿(綠)茶( )？
    - Nǐ xiǎng hē hóngchá háishì lǜchá?
    - Do you want to drink black tea or green tea?
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 艹
- **definition:** grass
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng cǎo zuò cǎozì de piānpáng.
    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.
    - When spring arrives, grass grows rapidly.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘)
- **traditional:** 餘
- **definition:** I; surplus
- **pinyin:** yú
- **components:**
  - 食
    - shí
    - food
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 食 (shí, 'food') + phonetic 余(餘) (yú, 'I') ->
    - remains of food ->
    - 'excess, surplus'
  - **interpretation:** The character 餘 evolved to represent the concept of 'excess' or 'surplus' through a phono-semantic compound. It combines the semantic component 食 (shí, 'food'), representing food or consumption, with the phonetic component 余(餘) (yú, 'I'), to indicate leftovers or remains of food. This directly suggests an aspect of having more than what is needed, thus an 'excess' or 'surplus'.
  - **simplification:** The traditional character 餘 was simplified to 余 by removing the semantic component 食 (shí, 'food'), leaving only the phonetic component.
- **examples:**
  - 余(餘)愿(願)以( )诗(詩)书(書)为(為)乐(樂)。
    - Yú yuàn yǐ shīshū wéi lè.
    - I wish to take pleasure in poetry and books.
  - 余(餘)在此地，心( )安( )吾( )归(歸)。
    - Yú zài cǐdì, xīn ān wú guī.
    - I am here, at peace and at home.
  - 余(餘)莫( )负(負)此( )生( )。
    - Yú mò fù cǐ shēng.
    - I should not waste this life.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 余](@media/glyph4f59.png)
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食
- **definition:** food
- **pinyin:** shí
- **components:**
  - 亼
    - jí
    - assemble
  - 𠊊
    - jí
    - bowl with stand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:** 亼 (jí, "assemble") over a pictographic bowl of rice on a stand, symbolized by 𠊊 (jí, "bowl with stand")
  - **interpretation:** Initially, the character 食 (shí, "eat") visually represented a mouth over a bowl of rice, conveying the act of eating or a meal intuitively through this imagery.
- **examples:**
  - 他食得很快。
    - Tā shí dé hěn kuài.
    - He eats very quickly.
  - 我( )准(準)备(備)午( )食( )，与(與)朋( )友( )共( )享( )佳( )肴(餚)。
    - Wǒ zhǔnbèi wǔ shí, yǔ péngyǒu gòngxiǎng jiāyáo.
    - I am preparing lunch to share a delicious meal with my friends.
  - 民( )以( )食( )为(為)天( )。
    - Mín yǐ shí wéi tiān.
    - Food is regarded as the most important element for the people.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 亼
- **definition:** assemble
- **pinyin:** jí
- **components:**
  - 入
    - rù
    - enter
  - 一
    - yī
    - one
  - 今
    - jīn
    - now
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 入 (rù, "enter") + 一 (yī, "one") =
    - gathering from three sides ->
    - original form of 今 (jīn, "now")
  - **interpretation:** 亼 (jī, "open mouth flipped upside down") originally represented the action of gathering, with 入 (rù, "enter") suggesting bringing things together and 一 (yī, "one") implying unification into a single entity. This concept aligns traditionally with 集 (jí, "gather"), where the sense of gathering from three sides connects to assembling or coming together. The visual comparison to an open mouth reversed is a less common interpretation, relating to a shape rather than function.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 亼](@media/glyph4ebc.png)
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 亼 → 入
- **definition:** enter
- **pinyin:** rù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** An arrowhead indicating 'to enter'
  - **interpretation:** The character 入(rù, 'enter') visually represents the concept of entering, with the form resembling an arrowhead suggesting movement through an opening.
- **examples:**
  - 请(請)入( )座( )。
    - Qǐng rùzuò.
    - Please take a seat.
  - 学(學)生( )走( )入( )校( )园(園)，开(開)始( )新( )学(學)期( )。
    - Xuéshēng zǒu rù xiàoyuán, kāishǐ xīn xuéqī.
    - The students enter the campus and start a new semester.
  - 游(遊)客( )走( )入( )古( )城( )，探( )索( )历(歷)史( )。
    - Yóukè zǒu rù gǔchéng, tànsuǒ lìshǐ.
    - The tourists enter the ancient city and explore its history.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 入](@media/glyph5165.png)
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 亼 → 一
- **definition:** one
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.
    - He only saw one bird, which flew into the garden.
  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.
    - One straight road leads to the distance.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 一](@media/glyph4e00.png)
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 亼 → 今
- **definition:** now
- **pinyin:** jīn
- **components:**
  - 曰
    - yuē
    - to speak
  - 亼
    - jí
    - collect; assemble
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 曰 (yuē, "to speak") inverted with 亼-like structure + horizontal stroke ->
    - original form implying 'not to speak' or 'keep in mouth'
  - **interpretation:** The character depicts an inverted version of 曰 (yuē, "to speak"), with a structure resembling 亼 and a horizontal stroke, representing something placed in the mouth, leading to the sense of 'keeping in the mouth' or 'not speaking'. This image of being contained or holding leads to 'now', as in holding or having the present moment.
- **examples:**
  - 今( )天( )的( )天( )气(氣)不( )错(錯)。
    - Jīntiān de tiānqì bùcuò.
    - Today's weather is nice.
  - 现(現)在( )是( )吃( )晚( )饭(飯)的( )时(時)候( )。
    - Xiànzài shì chī wǎnfàn de shíhòu.
    - Now is the time to have dinner.
  - 她( )今( )天( )特( )别(別)忙( )，没(沒)时(時)间(間)见(見)面( )。
    - Tā jīntiān tèbié máng, méi shíjiān jiànmiàn.
    - She is especially busy today and doesn't have time to meet.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 𠊊
- **definition:** bowl with stand
- **pinyin:** jí
- **components:**
  - 亼
    - jí
    - mouth
  - 皀
    - jí
    - bowl of rice on a stand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 亼 (mouth) over 皀 (bowl of rice on a stand) ->
    - depicts a bowl with a stand
  - **interpretation:** The character 𠊊 visually represents a mouth above a bowl of rice on a stand, indicating a utensil or vessel used for holding food. This image directly links to the idea of a 'bowl with stand.' The component 皀 specifically conveys the idea of a container or bowl elevated, which situates its primary meaning in the context of serving or holding food.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 𠊊 → 皀
- **definition:** bowl of rice on a stand
- **pinyin:** jí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a bowl of rice on a stand ->
    - part of the radical 食 (shí, "food")
  - **interpretation:** The character 皀(bá, "acorn") depicts a bowl of rice on a stand, aligning it with food-related imagery.

## 卫(衛)生( )
---
## hygiene
### wèishēng
---
- **traditional:** 衛生
- **definition:** hygiene
- **pinyin:** wèishēng
- **components:**
  - 卫(衛)
    - wèi
    - guard; protect; defend
  - 生
    - shēng
    - life; health; live; raw
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 卫(衛) (wèi, "guard") + 生 (shēng, "life") =
    - guarding life ->
    - hygiene
  - **interpretation:** Hygiene involves practices that guard or protect one's health and life by promoting cleanliness and preventing disease. In this context, 卫(衛) (wèi, "guard") implies protection, while 生 (shēng, "life") refers to health and life itself.
  - **simplification:** 卫生 is the simplified form of 衛生.
- **examples:**
  - 保( )持( )卫(衛)生( )很( )重( )要( )。
    - Bǎochí wèishēng hěn zhòngyào.
    - Maintaining hygiene is important.
  - 在( )餐( )厅(廳)用( )餐( )前( )，请(請)检(檢)查( )卫(衛)生( )。
    - Zài cāntīng yòngcān qián, qǐng jiǎnchá wèishēng.
    - Before dining in the restaurant, please check the hygiene.
  - 环(環)境( )卫(衛)生( )关(關)系(係)到( )健( )康( )。
    - Huánjìng wèishēng guānxì dào jiànkāng.
    - Environmental hygiene is related to health.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛)
- **traditional:** 衛
- **definition:** guard; protect; defend
- **pinyin:** wèi
- **components:**
  - 行
    - xíng
    - walk
  - 韋
    - wéi
    - tanned leather
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 行 (xíng, 'walk') + phonetic 韋 (wéi, 'tanned leather') ->
    - guard; protect; defend
  - **interpretation:** The character 衛 (wèi, 'guard; protect; defend') combines the semantic element 行 (xíng, 'walk'), which suggests movement or patrolling, with the phonetic component 韋 (wéi, 'tanned leather'), which provides the pronunciation. The imagery of a person moving while wearing protective leather gear conveys the sense of guarding or defending an area.
  - **simplification:** The traditional form 衛 was simplified to 卫 by reducing it to a minimal structure that phonetically resembles the compound it was simplified from. The complex parts were replaced by a form influenced by certain katakana elements (as in Japanese writing), a process unrelated to preserving meaning but retaining phonetic recognition.
- **examples:**
  - 保( )安( )在( )大( )楼(樓)门(門)口( )守( )卫(衛)。
    - Bǎo'ān zài dàlóu ménkǒu shǒuwèi.
    - The security guard is stationed at the entrance of the building.
  - 小( )王( )负(負)责(責)卫(衛)士( )的( )工( )作( )。
    - Xiǎo Wáng fùzé wèishì de gōngzuò.
    - Xiao Wang is responsible for the work of the guard.
  - 我( )们(們)要( )卫(衛)国(國)，保家。
    - Wǒmen yào wèi guó, bǎo jiā.
    - We need to defend the country and protect the home.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** xíng
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - step
  - 亍
    - chù
    - step
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally a symmetric depiction of a street intersection; simplified asymmetrically ->
    - 彳 (chì, "step") + 亍 (chù, "step")
  - **interpretation:** The character 行(xíng) visually represented a street intersection, with two symmetrical parts symbolizing the act of going or walking along paths. Despite its current breakdown into separate elements, it was originally a unified pictogram rather than a compound.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )行( )往( )学(學)校( )。
    - Wǒ xíng wǎng xuéxiào.
    - I go to school.
  - 他( )行( )于(於)青( )山( )之( )间(間)，徒步旅行至古城。
    - Tā xíng yú qīngshān zhī jiān, túbù lǚxíng zhì gǔchéng.
    - He walks among the green mountains and hikes to the ancient city.
  - 他( )们(們)行( )在( )林( )间(間)小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tāmen xíng zài línjiān xiǎolù shàng.
    - They walk on the forest trail.
- **historical forms:**

### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行 → 彳
- **definition:** step
- **pinyin:** chì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a foot or person walking
  - **interpretation:** 彳(chì, 'walking') visually represents steps or a person moving, suggesting the idea of walking or movement. Despite a simplified history from characters like 行(xíng, 'walk'), its pictographic heritage directly connects with the notion of stepping or proceeding.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)在( )河( )边(邊)慢( )慢( )地( )走( )。
    - Tā xǐhuān zài hé biān mànmàn de zǒu.
    - He likes to walk slowly by the river.
  - 我( )们(們)每( )天( )退( )出( )喧( )嚣(囂)，享( )受( )安( )静(靜)的( )散( )步( )。
    - Wǒmen měitiān tuìchū xuānxiāo, xiǎngshòu ānjìng de sànbù.
    - We withdraw from the hustle and bustle every day to enjoy a quiet walk.
  - 他( )常( )常( )独(獨)自( )彳( )亍( )于(於)公( )园(園)小( )径(徑)。
    - Tā chángcháng dúzì chìchù yú gōngyuán xiǎojìng.
    - He often walks alone on the park trails.

### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 韋
- **definition:** tanned leather
- **pinyin:** wéi
- **components:**
  - 舛
    - chuǎn
    - footsteps
  - 囗
    - wéi
    - enclosure
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 舛 (footsteps) + 囗 (enclosure) ->
    - footsteps around an enclosure ->
    - originally for 圍 (to surround); phonetically borrowed for 'soft leather'
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 舛 (footsteps) and 囗 (enclosure) to depict the idea of footsteps moving around an enclosed area, which was originally associated with the concept of surrounding. This shape was later borrowed for its phonetic value to mean 'soft leather,' as the structural composition resembles a process of encasing or wrapping, similar to the treatment of leather.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)件( )夹(夾)克( )是( )用( )韦(韋)材( )料( )制(製)成( )的( )。
    - Zhè jiàn jiákè shì yòng wéi cáiliào zhìchéng de.
    - This jacket is made from soft leather material.
  - 古( )代( )的( )盔( )甲( )常( )以( )韦(韋)作( )装(裝)饰(飾)。
    - Gǔdài de kuījiǎ cháng yǐ wéi zuò zhuāngshì.
    - Ancient armor was often decorated with soft leather.
  - 他( )的( )书(書)包( )用( )韦(韋)边(邊)制(製)成( )。
    - Tā de shūbāo yòng wéi biān zhìchéng.
    - His backpack is made with soft leather edges.

### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 韋 → 舛
- **definition:** footsteps
- **pinyin:** chuǎn
- **components:**
  - 夊
    - suī
    - foot; walk slowly
  - 𡕒
    - wěi
    - foot; part of an ancient form
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 夊("foot") + 𡕒("foot") =
    - two feet facing the same direction
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicted two feet facing the same direction, indicative of alignment or a specific movement. This contrasts with similar characters where feet face different directions, suggesting divergence or a change in direction. The character captures the concept of feet in synchronized motion, highlighting its thematic link to physical orientation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )计(計)划(劃)总(總)是( )出( )点(點)儿(兒)小( )舛( )。
    - Tā de jìhuà zǒng shì chū diǎnr xiǎo chuǎn.
    - His plans always encounter small setbacks.
  - 尽(儘)管( )有( )舛( )错(錯)，他( )仍( )然( )达(達)到( )了( )目( )标(標)。
    - Jǐnguǎn yǒu chuǎncuò, tā réngrán dádào le mùbiāo.
    - Despite the mistakes, he still achieved his goal.
  - 为(為)了( )避( )免( )舛( )误(誤)，我( )们(們)仔( )细(細)检(檢)查( )了( )所( )有( )的( )数(數)据(據)。
    - Wèile bìmiǎn chuǎnwù, wǒmen zǐxì jiǎnchá le suǒyǒu de shùjù.
    - To avoid errors, we carefully checked all the data.

### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 韋 → 舛 → 夊 → 夂

  - **description:** Depicts a footprint pointing down
  - **interpretation:** The character 夂 (zhǐ, 'foot pointing down') is a pictogram representing a footprint facing downward, often associated with motion or directionality. This depiction intuitively aligns with its meaning of indicating a downward step or path.
- **examples:**

    - Hànzì zhōng ‘zhǐ’ cháng bèi yòng zuò piān páng, xiàng zhēng bù fá cháo xià.
    - In Chinese characters, '夂' is often used as a radical to symbolize downward steps.
  - 书(書)法( )中( )‘夂( )’的( )结(結)构(構)犹(猶)如( )足( )尖( )向( )下( )。

### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 韋 → 舛 → 𡕒
- **definition:** foot; part of an ancient form
- **pinyin:** wěi
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 夸
    - kuā
    - boast
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 足 (zú, "foot") + phonetic 夸 (kuā) ->
    - meaning relates to feet
  - **interpretation:** The character 𡕒 is a variant of 跨, which is constructed from the semantic component 足 (zú, "foot"), indicating its relation to activities involving feet, and the phonetic component 夸 (kuā). This combination suggests the notion of actions involving feet, such as 'stride' or 'step over', derived from the sound provided by 夸.

    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。

  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。

### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 韋 → 舛 → 𡕒 → 夸(誇)

  - **description:**
    - semantic 言 (yán, "speech") + phonetic 夸 (kuā, "boast") ->
    - boastful speech
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 言 (yán, "speech") for its semantic value, suggesting something spoken or related to words, with 夸 (kuā, "boast") providing the pronunciation. The result is a word associated with boastful or exaggerated speech.
  - **simplification:** The traditional 言 (yán, "speech") was removed, leaving only the component 夸 (kuā, "boast") as the simplified character.
- **examples:**

    - Tā kuājiǎng zìjǐ zài bǐsài zhōng de biǎoxiàn.
    - She boasted about her performance in the competition.

    - The teacher boasted about his great progress.

## 江( )山( )如( )画(畫)
---
## picturesque scenery
### jiāngshān rúhuà
---
- **traditional:** 江山如畫
- **definition:** picturesque scenery
- **pinyin:** jiāngshān rúhuà
- **components:**
  - 江
    - jiāng
    - river
  - 山
    - shān
    - mountain
  - 如
    - rú
    - like; as
  - 畫
    - huà
    - painting; drawing
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 江 (jiāng, "river") refers to a large waterway, often symbolizing nature's grandeur. 山 (shān, "mountain") signifies elevated landforms, representing stability and natural majesty. 如 (rú, "like/as") is a comparison marker, highlighting similarity. 畫 (huà, "painting") denotes an artistic depiction. Combined, this word evokes the image of a landscape so beautiful it resembles a painting, emphasizing picturesque or idyllic natural scenery.
  - **interpretation:** 江 (jiāng, "river") and 山 (shān, "mountain") together describe natural landscapes. 如 (rú, "like") establishes a simile, indicating a resemblance. 畫 (huà, "painting") suggests an artistic quality. The term implies a natural scene so strikingly beautiful it could be a crafted artwork. The idea links aesthetic appreciation of nature to the refined art of painting, underscoring both the beauty of the landscape and the creator's artistic sensibility.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)片( )土( )地( )江( )山( )如( )画(畫)。
    - Zhè piàn tǔdì jiāngshān rúhuà.
    - This land has picturesque scenery.
  - 漫( )步( )在( )公( )园(園)里(裡)，我( )感( )叹(嘆)江( )山( )如( )画(畫)。
    - Mànbù zài gōngyuán lǐ, wǒ gǎntàn jiāngshān rúhuà.
    - Walking in the park, I marveled at the picturesque scenery.
  - 游(遊)客( )们(們)被( )这(這)里(裡)的( )江( )山( )如( )画(畫)所( )吸( )引( )。
    - Yóukèmen bèi zhèlǐ de jiāngshān rúhuà suǒ xīyǐn.
    - The tourists were attracted by the picturesque scenery here.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 江
- **definition:** river
- **pinyin:** jiāng
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 工
    - gōng
    - work
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 工 (gōng) ->
    - meaning
  - **interpretation:** The water radical 氵 (shuǐ, "water") indicates the association with water, specifically a river. The 工 (gōng) provides the pronunciation, connecting the sound with the natural context of water courses.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)条(條)江( )流( )经(經)许(許)多( )城( )镇(鎮)。
    - Zhè tiáo jiāng liújīng xǔduō chéngzhèn.
    - This river flows through many towns.
  - 江( )水( )捧( )起( )一( )片( )叶(葉)子( )，飘(飄)向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Jiāngshuǐ pěng qǐ yī piàn yèzi, piāo xiàng yuǎnfāng.
    - The river water lifted a leaf, floating it into the distance.
  - 我( )喜( )欢(歡)在( )江( )边(邊)散( )步( )，欣( )赏(賞)美( )景( )。
    - Wǒ xǐhuān zài jiāng biān sànbù, xīnshǎng měijǐng.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 江 → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 江 → 工
- **definition:** work
- **pinyin:** gōng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bladed tool to plow the ground and to mark a border, or a carpenter's square
  - **interpretation:** 工 (gōng, "work") represents either a tool used in agriculture for marking or a carpenter's square, reflecting its connection to traditional manual labor and craftsmanship.
- **examples:**
  - 他每天下午去做工。
    - Tā měitiān xiàwǔ qù zuò gōng.
    - He goes to work every afternoon.
  - 工( )厂(廠)里(裡)的( )机(機)器( )不( )停( )地( )运(運)转(轉)。
    - Gōng chǎng lǐ de jīqì bù tíng de yùnzhuǎn.
    - The machines in the factory run nonstop.
  - 这(這)位( )工( )人( )技( )艺(藝)精( )湛( )。
    - Zhè wèi gōngrén jìyì jīngzhàn.
    - This worker is highly skilled.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 山
- **definition:** mountain

- **etymology:**

  - **description:** Depicts three mountain peaks
  - **interpretation:** The character 山(shān, "mountain") visually represents the concept of a mountain using three vertical lines that suggest peaks. This depiction is consistent with early forms found in Oracle Bone Script, emphasizing the notion of natural elevation associated with mountains.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)去( )爬( )山( )。
    - Wǒmen qù pá shān.
    - We go climb the mountain.
  - 那( )座( )大( )山( )让(讓)人( )惊(驚)叹(嘆)。
    - Nà zuò dà shān ràng rén jīngtàn.
    - That mountain astonishes people.
  - 远(遠)处(處)的( )山( )隐(隱)约(約)在( )云(雲)中( )，令( )人( )心( )旷(曠)神( )怡( )。
    - Yuǎnchù de shān yǐnyuē zài yún zhōng, lìng rén xīnkuàng shényí.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 如
- **definition:** like; as
- **pinyin:** rú
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - woman
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 女 (nǚ, "woman") ->
    - like/as if
  - **interpretation:** The character 如(rú, "like; as if") combines 口 (kǒu, "mouth") as the semantic element, suggesting speech or expression, with 女 (nǚ, "woman") as the phonetic element indicating the sound. The woman's compliance in cultural contexts implies conformity or likeness, leading to meanings like 'like' or 'as if'.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )如( )风(風)一( )般( )快( )速( )穿( )过(過)人( )群( )。
    - Tā rú fēng yī bān kuài sù chuān guò rén qún.
    - He moved through the crowd as swiftly as the wind.
  - 他( )的( )声(聲)音( )如( )天( )籁(籟)，令人陶醉。
    - Tā de shēng yīn rú tiān lài, lìng rén táo zuì.
    - His voice was like celestial music, intoxicating to the listener.
  - 她( )的( )笑( )容( )如( )阳(陽)光( )般( )灿(燦)烂(爛)。
    - Tā de xiào róng rú yáng guāng bān càn làn.
    - Her smile was as bright as the sunlight.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 如 → 口
- **definition:** mouth

- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 如 → 女
- **definition:** woman

- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**

    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫
- **definition:** painting; drawing

- **components:**
  - 聿

  - 規
    - guī
    - compass
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:** semantic 聿 (yù, "brush") + phonetic 規 (guī, "compass")
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 聿 (yù, "brush") as the semantic component, signifying the connection to writing or drawing, with 規 (guī, "compass") as the phonetic component to provide the sound. The use of a brush underscores the association with painting or drawing, depicting the action or art of making images with a brush.
- **examples:**
  - 他正在绘制一幅美丽的画(畫)。
    - Tā zhèngzài huìzhì yī fú měilì de huà.

    - Háizimen xǐhuān yòng làbǐ huà, chuàngzuò zìjǐ de gùshì.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 聿
- **definition:** brush
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
  - 𰀁
    - yù
    - brush
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 手 (shǒu, 'hand') stylized as 又 (yòu, 'hand') + 𰀁 (yù, 'brush') =
    - hand holding a brush
  - **interpretation:** The character 聿(yù) originally depicted a hand holding a brush, symbolizing the act of writing or drawing. It was initially used for the writing brush itself and later borrowed for other meanings.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )代( )文( )人( )以( )聿( )书(書)写(寫)心( )志( )。
    - Gǔdài wénrén yǐ yù shūxiě xīnzhì.
    - Ancient literati used the brush to write their aspirations.
  - 书(書)法( )之( )美( )在( )于(於)用( )聿( )，笔(筆)随(隨)心( )动(動)。
    - Shūfǎ zhī měi zàiyú yòng yù, bǐ suí xīndòng.
    - The beauty of calligraphy lies in using a brush, as the brush follows the movements of the heart.
  - 他( )用( )聿( )写(寫)出( )了( )一( )篇( )优(優)美( )的( )诗(詩)。
    - Tā yòng yù xiě chūle yī piān yōuměi de shī.
    - He used the brush to compose a beautiful poem.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 聿 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**

    - He writes with his hand.

    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.

- **etymology:**

  - **description:** Depicts a hand holding a brush or writing implement
  - **interpretation:** The character 聿 (yù, "brush") visually represents a hand grasping a writing tool, which is suggestive of its association with writing or drawing actions.
- **examples:**
  - 书(書)法( )家( )拿( )起( )𰀁( )，挥(揮)洒(灑)时(時)代( )风(風)采( )。

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規
- **definition:** compass
- **pinyin:** guī
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - husband
  - 癸
    - guǐ
    - tenth heavenly stem
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Originally a pictogram of a compass ->
    - transformed to combine 夫 (fū, "husband") + a phonetic component similar to 癸
  - **interpretation:** The character 規 (guī, "compass") originally depicted a cross-shaped measuring compass. Over time, it evolved to incorporate elements for phonetic reasons—specifically, adopting an abbreviated form of the phonetic component 癸 (guǐ, "tenth heavenly stem"). The association with 夫 (fū, "husband") originally related to the tool-like function of a compass in measurement.
  - **simplification:** 规(規): 見 component was removed and replaced with 见, a simpler form, preserving the phonetic similarity.
- **examples:**
  - 老( )师(師)教( )我( )们(們)如( )何( )使( )用( )规(規)。
    - Lǎoshī jiāo wǒmen rúhé shǐyòng guī.
    - The teacher teaches us how to use a compass.
  - 建( )筑(築)师(師)利( )用( )规(規)和( )尺( )来(來)绘(繪)制(製)蓝(藍)图(圖)。
    - Jiànzhùshī lìyòng guī hé chǐ lái huìzhì lántú.
    - The architect uses a compass and ruler to draft blueprints.
  - 古( )代( )的( )工( )匠( )依( )靠( )简(簡)单(單)的( )规(規)和( )工( )具( )制(製)作( )精( )美( )的( )器( )物( )。
    - Gǔdài de gōngjiàng yīkào jiǎndān de guī hé gōngjù zhìzuò jīngměi de qìwù.
    - Ancient artisans relied on simple compasses and tools to craft exquisite artifacts.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 夫
- **definition:** husband
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 夫 → 大
- **definition:** big
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 癸
- **definition:** tenth heavenly stem
- **pinyin:** guǐ
- **components:**
  - 戣
    - kuí
    - halberd
  - 葵
    - kuí
    - mallow
  - 規
    - guī
    - compass; rule
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 癸 depicts a form whose original meaning is uncertain ->
    - associated with measuring tools or setups for determining directions and solstices ->
    - possible original forms of 戣 (kuí, "halberd"), 葵 (kuí, "mallow"), 規 (guī, "compass; rule")
  - **interpretation:** The character 癸 (guǐ, "tenth earthly branch") is a pictogram whose depiction is uncertain but is conceptually linked to measurement or orientation tools. These tools were possibly used for solar observations or direction finding, thus lending the character significance in calendrical or positional contexts.
- **examples:**
  - 在( )中( )国(國)古( )代( )历(曆)法( )中( )，癸( )象( )征(徵)着(著)第( )十( )个(個)时(時)间(間)段( )。
    - Zài Zhōngguó gǔdài lìfǎ zhōng, guǐ xiàngzhēng zhe dì shí gè shíjiān duàn.
    - In traditional Chinese calendrical systems, guǐ symbolizes the tenth time period.
  - 古( )代( )学(學)者( )通( )过(過)观(觀)察( )天( )象( )，用( )癸( )来(來)记(記)录(錄)岁(歲)月( )更( )迭( )。
    - Gǔdài xuézhě tōngguò guānchá tiānxiàng, yòng guǐ lái jìlù suìyuè gēngdié.
    - Ancient scholars, by observing celestial phenomena, used guǐ to record the passage of time.
  - 天( )干( )地( )支( )系( )统(統)里( )，癸( )代( )表( )第( )十( )个(個)地( )支( )。
    - Tiāngān dìzhī xìtǒng lǐ, guǐ dàibiǎo dì shí gè dìzhī.
    - In the heavenly stems and earthly branches system, guǐ represents the tenth earthly branch.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 癸 → 戣

    - wèi
    - a Warring State; high

  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 戈 (gē, "halberd") + phonetic 魏 (wèi) ->
    - sounding kuí, weapon type
  - **interpretation:** The character 戣 (kuí, "halberd") combines the semantic component 戈 (gē, "halberd") which directly refers to a type of weapon, with the phonetic component 魏 (wèi) to provide the sound kuí. The character specifically denotes a kind of halberd, inheriting its physical association from the戈 component.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 癸 → 戣 → 戈

  - **description:** Depicts a spear or halberd, a type of weapon

  - 博( )物( )馆(館)珍( )藏( )古( )兵( )器( )，展( )厅(廳)陈(陳)列( )戈( )。
    - Bówùguǎn zhēncáng gǔ bīngqì, zhǎntīng chénliè gē.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 癸 → 戣 → 魏
- **definition:** a Warring State; high
- **pinyin:** wèi
- **components:**
  - 委
    - wěi
    - entrust
  - 鬼
    - guǐ
    - ghost
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - phonetic 委 + phonetic 鬼 ->
    - 魏
  - **interpretation:** The character 魏 (wèi), referring to the historical state, combines phonetic elements 委 (wěi, 'entrust') and 鬼 (guǐ, 'ghost'), with no direct semantic contribution from either component. The usage for the state of Wei relates primarily through phonetic borrowing rather than inherent meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 在( )战(戰)国(國)时(時)期( )，魏( )国(國)是( )七( )雄( )之( )一( )。
    - Zài Zhànguó shíqī, Wèiguó shì qīxióng zhī yī.
    - During the Warring States period, the State of Wei was one of the Seven Heroes.
  - 魏( )武( )王( )是( )一( )个(個)有( )名( )的( )统(統)治( )者( )。
    - Wèi Wǔ Wáng shì yī gè yǒumíng de tǒngzhìzhě.
    - King Wei Wu was a famous ruler.
  - 这(這)个(個)山( )峰( )耸(聳)立( )在( )云(雲)端( )之( )上( )，显(顯)得( )格( )外( )雄( )伟(偉)。
    - Zhège shānfēng sǒnglì zài yúnduān zhī shàng, xiǎnde géwài xióngwěi.
    - This mountain peak stands tall above the clouds, appearing exceptionally majestic.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 癸 → 葵
- **definition:** mallow
- **pinyin:** kuí
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass; plant
  - 癸
    - guǐ
    - tenth heavenly stem
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 艹("grass; plant") + phonetic 癸(sound) ->
    - a kind of plant
  - **interpretation:** The character 葵 combines the semantic component 艹, indicating 'grass' or 'plant', with the phonetic component 癸, which provides the sound. This structure denotes a kind of plant, which can include mallow, sunflower, or hibiscus based on context.
- **examples:**
  - 花( )园(園)中( )种(種)满(滿)了( )向( )日( )葵( )。

  - 在古代，葵( )花( )常( )被( )用( )作( )药(藥)材( )。
    - Zài gǔdài, kuíhuā cháng bèi yòng zuò yàocái.
    - In ancient times, the mallow flower was often used as medicine.
  - 清晨的露水，使( )葵( )花( )显(顯)得( )更( )加( )美( )丽(麗)。
    - Qīngchén de lùshuǐ, shǐ kuíhuā xiǎnde gèngjiā měilì.

### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 畫 → 規 → 癸 → 葵 → 艹
- **definition:** grass; plant
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。

    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.

## 运(運)动(動)
---
## exercise; sports
### yùndòng
---
- **traditional:** 運動
- **definition:** exercise; sports
- **pinyin:** yùndòng
- **components:**
  - 运(運)
    - yùn
    - to transport; fortune; motion; luck
  - 动(動)
    - dòng
    - to move; movement; action; motion
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 運 (yùn, "to transport; fortune") refers to movement or transportation, which extends to imply activity. 動 (dòng, "to move; movement") reinforces the theme of motion or activity.
  - **interpretation:** 運 (yùn, "to transport; fortune") originally meant the act of carrying or moving something from one place to another, thus denoting activity and motion. 動 (dòng, "to move; movement") signifies physical movement or change in position, adding to the idea of actively engaging in physical activities. Together, the term 運動 encompasses physical exercises and sports as activities involving coordinated movement and effort.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )每( )天( )早( )晨( )去( )公( )园(園)运(運)动(動)。
    - Wǒ měitiān zǎochén qù gōngyuán yùndòng.
    - I go to the park to exercise every morning.
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)参(參)加( )各( )种(種)运(運)动(動)。
    - Tā xǐhuān cānjiā gè zhǒng yùndòng.
    - He likes to participate in various sports.
  - 运(運)动(動)对(對)健( )康( )有( )益( )，可( )以( )让(讓)人( )保( )持( )活( )力( )。
    - Yùndòng duì jiànkāng yǒuyì, kěyǐ ràng rén bǎochí huólì.
    - Exercise is beneficial for health and can help people stay energetic.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運)
- **traditional:** 運
- **definition:** to transport; fortune; motion; luck
- **pinyin:** yùn
- **components:**
  - 辵
    - chuò
    - walk; movement
  - 軍
    - jūn
    - army
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 辵 (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 軍 (jūn, "army") ->
    - military movement ->
    - transport
  - **interpretation:** The character 運 represents the concept of moving or transporting something, as the 軍 (jūn, "army") provides the sound while 辵 (chuò, "walk; movement") signifies the action of moving. This aligns with the sense of 'transport' and 'move'. 'Fortune' and 'luck' are metaphorically related as the movement of chance or fate.
  - **simplification:** 运 is the simplified form of 運, where 運 was simplified by omitting the 辵 radical's details, retaining the 軛 (è, "yoke") for its phonetic role.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)的( )货(貨)物( )运(運)到( )了( )。
    - Wǒmen de huòwù yùn dào le.
    - Our goods have been transported.
  - 最( )近( )我( )的( )运(運)气(氣)很( )好( )。
    - Zuìjìn wǒ de yùnqì hěn hǎo.
    - Recently, my luck has been good.
  - 灯(燈)光( )可( )以( )改( )变(變)房( )间(間)的( )气(氣)氛( )，营(營)造( )好( )运(運)的( )感( )觉(覺)。
    - Dēngguāng kěyǐ gǎibiàn fángjiān de qìfēn, yíngzào hǎoyùn de gǎnjué.
    - Lighting can change the atmosphere of a room, creating a sense of good fortune.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵
- **definition:** walk; movement
- **pinyin:** chuò
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - walk
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 彳 (chì, "walk") + 止 (zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop") ->
    - 𣥆 ->
    - stylized as 龰 ->
    - 辵
  - **interpretation:** The character 辵 combines 彳 (chì, "walk"), which represents movement, with 止 (zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop"), suggesting the concept of stepping or traveling forward. This character represents the idea of movement, particularly the action of walking or traveling, as originally depicted by the more conservative form 𣥆. Over time, the bottom component has been stylized as 龰, forming the current representation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )辵( )在( )乡(鄉)间(間)的( )小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tā chuò zài xiāngjiān de xiǎolù shàng.
    - He walks on the rural path.
  - 晨( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )，他( )辵( )出( )门(門)开(開)始( )旅( )程( )。
    - Chén guāng sǎ mǎn dàdì, tā chuò chūmén kāishǐ lǚchéng.
    - As the morning light fills the earth, he sets out to begin his journey.
- **historical forms:**

### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 彳
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** chì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a foot or person walking
  - **interpretation:** 彳(chì, 'walking') visually represents steps or a person moving, suggesting the idea of walking or movement. Despite a simplified history from characters like 行(xíng, 'walk'), its pictographic heritage directly connects with the notion of stepping or proceeding.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)在( )河( )边(邊)慢( )慢( )地( )走( )。
    - Tā xǐhuān zài hé biān mànmàn de zǒu.
    - He likes to walk slowly by the river.
  - 我( )们(們)每( )天( )退( )出( )喧( )嚣(囂)，享( )受( )安( )静(靜)的( )散( )步( )。
    - Wǒmen měitiān tuìchū xuānxiāo, xiǎngshòu ānjìng de sànbù.
    - We withdraw from the hustle and bustle every day to enjoy a quiet walk.
  - 他( )常( )常( )独(獨)自( )彳( )亍( )于(於)公( )园(園)小( )径(徑)。
    - Tā chángcháng dúzì chìchù yú gōngyuán xiǎojìng.
    - He often walks alone on the park trails.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 彳](@media/glyph5f73.png)
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.
    - He walks carefully because if his foot gets injured, it becomes difficult to move forward.

### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - halt
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 足 (zú, "foot") + phonetic 止 (zhǐ) ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot"), indicating the general area involved, with 止 (zhǐ) providing the sound and potentially suggesting the idea of a part of the foot that 'halts' or 'stops' progression, which naturally leads to the notion of a toe.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )脚(腳)趾( )受( )伤(傷)了( )。
    - Tā de jiǎozhǐ shòushāng le.
    - His toe is injured.
  - 她( )用( )脚(腳)趾( )轻(輕)轻(輕)地( )踢( )了( )一( )下( )球( )。
    - Tā yòng jiǎozhǐ qīngqīng de tī le yīxià qiú.
    - She gently kicked the ball with her toe.
  - 在( )沙( )滩(灘)上( )散( )步( )时(時)，我( )感( )受( )到( )沙( )子( )在( )我( )的( )脚(腳)趾( )间(間)流( )动(動)。
    - Zài shātān shàng sànbù shí, wǒ gǎnshòu dào shāzi zài wǒ de jiǎozhǐ jiān liúdòng.
    - While walking on the beach, I felt the sand flowing between my toes.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.

### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a stylized footprint pointing upward ->
    - originally meaning 'foot' ->
    - later forms like 趾 (zhǐ, 'toe') refer back to original meaning
  - **interpretation:** 龰 (yù, 'stylized feet') is a pictogram showing a footprint pointing up, originally indicating the concept of a foot. The character 趾 (zhǐ, 'toe') evolved from this and retains the reference to the original meaning. It visually resembles a sole with three toes, similar to representations of the human hand in characters like 爪 (zhuǎ, 'claw'), 寸 (cùn, 'inch'), and 又 (yòu, 'again').
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.

### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 軍
- **definition:** army
- **pinyin:** jūn
- **components:**
  - 車
    - chē
    - chariot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 車 (chē, "chariot") + phonetic component related to original forms of 螾 (yǐn, "earthworm"), 旬 (xún, "ten days"), 勻 (yún, "even"), 云 (yún, "say") ->
    - formation of an organized group or army
  - **interpretation:** The character 軍(jūn) represents a military unit, consisting of a chariot 車 (chē, "chariot") as the semantic component, indicating mobility and equipment, paired with a phonetic component that historically is tied to concepts of organization and grouping, implying a unified military force.
  - **simplification:** The simplified form 军 reduces the character to essential strokes, maintaining the structure representing chariot imagery while resembling a fortification or group lining.
- **examples:**
  - 军(軍)队(隊)正( )在( )训(訓)练(練)。
    - Jūnduì zhèngzài xùnliàn.
    - The army is training.
  - 他( )们(們)决(決)定( )加( )入( )军(軍)队(隊)。
    - Tāmen juédìng jiārù jūnduì.
    - They decided to join the army.
  - 古( )代( )的( )军(軍)队(隊)使( )用( )弓( )箭( )，而( )不( )是( )现(現)代( )的( )武( )器( )。
    - Gǔdài de jūnduì shǐyòng gōngjiàn, ér bùshì xiàndài de wǔqì.
    - Ancient armies used bows and arrows, not modern weapons.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 軍 → 車
- **definition:** chariot
- **pinyin:** chē
- **components:**
  - 田
    - tián
    - field
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a carriage viewed from above; wheels on both sides + sun shade on top ->
    - depicted vertically with wheels as strokes and loading area marked by 田 (tián, "field") ->
    - 車
  - **interpretation:** The character 車(chē, "vehicle") originally depicts a carriage from an overhead perspective. The transformation into the vertical form involved simplifying the depiction of wheels and emphasizing the loading area, marked by a field symbol, creating a streamlined pictographic representation.
  - **simplification:** The traditional character 車 was simplified to 车 by reducing the number of strokes, maintaining the overall shape of the vehicle but removing intricate details.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )坐( )车(車)去( )上( )班( )。
    - Wǒ zuò chē qù shàngbān.
    - I take a car to go to work.
  - 这(這)辆(輛)车(車)很( )快( )。
    - Zhè liàng chē hěn kuài.
    - This car is very fast.
  - 我( )想( )买(買)一( )辆(輛)新( )车(車)。
    - Wǒ xiǎng mǎi yī liàng xīn chē.
    - I want to buy a new car.

- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )在( )田( )里(裡)耕( )作( )。
    - Nóngmín zài tián lǐ gēngzuò.

    - Nàbiān de tián cháng mǎn le zhuāngjià, nóngmín shōuhuò mánglù.

### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動)
- **traditional:** 動
- **definition:** to move; movement; action; motion
- **pinyin:** dòng
- **components:**
  - 力
    - lì
    - strength
  - 重
    - zhòng
    - heavy
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 力 (lì, "strength") + phonetic 重 (zhòng) ->
    - movement aided by strength ->
    - move
  - **interpretation:** The character 動 suggests using strength 力 (lì, "strength") to move something heavy, as indicated by the phonetic component 重 (zhòng, "heavy"). This conveys the concept of movement or change.
  - **simplification:** The character 動 was simplified by replacing 重 (zhòng, "heavy") with 云 (yún, "cloud") to form 动, reducing the complexity of writing.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )动(動)了( )一( )下( )椅( )子( )。
    - Tā dòng le yīxià yǐzi.
    - He moved the chair slightly.
  - 我( )的( )心( )被( )她( )的( )话(話)感( )动(動)了( )。
    - Wǒ de xīn bèi tā de huà gǎn dòng le.
    - My heart was moved by her words.
  - 天( )气(氣)一( )变(變)动(動)，就( )会(會)下( )雨( )。
    - Tiānqì yī biàn dòng, jiù huì xià yǔ.
    - As soon as the weather changes, it will rain.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 力
- **definition:** strength
- **pinyin:** lì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts either an arm bending out from the side of the body, or a wooden plough
  - **interpretation:** The character 力(lì, 'strength') visually represents physical power through the image of an arm or tool, emphasizing the fundamental concept of muscular force.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )尽(盡)全( )力( )，赢(贏)得( )比( )赛(賽)。
    - Tā yòng jìn quánlì, yíng dé bǐsài.
    - He used all his strength to win the competition.
  - 他( )把( )力( )气(氣)用( )在( )学(學)习(習)上( )。
    - Tā bǎ lìqì yòng zài xuéxí shàng.
    - He applies his strength to his studies.
  - 面( )对(對)困( )难(難)，他( )毫( )不( )退( )缩(縮)，全力以赴。
    - Miànduì kùnnán, tā háo bù tuìsuō, quánlì yǐ fù.

### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重
- **definition:** heavy
- **pinyin:** zhòng
- **components:**
  - 人
    - rén
    - man
  - 東
    - dōng
    - bag
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - earth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 人 (rén, "man") + 東 (dōng, "bag") =
    - a man carrying a bag ->
    - 土 (tǔ, "earth") added ->
    - symbol of load ->
    - final form
  - **interpretation:** Originally, it depicts a man carrying a bag, indicating the concept of weight. With 土 (tǔ, "earth") added to signify being grounded, it amplifies the idea of heaviness due to the man's connection to the ground or earth, suggesting substantial weight or burden. This visual representation naturally evolved into "heavy", as a person burdened by earth is weighed down.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)箱( )苹(蘋)果( )很( )重( )。
    - Zhè xiāng píngguǒ hěn zhòng.
    - This box of apples is very heavy.
  - 他的行李太重，无(無)法( )一( )个(個)人( )搬( )动(動)。
    - Tā de xíngli tài zhòng, wúfǎ yīgè rén bāndòng.
    - His luggage is too heavy to carry alone.
  - 雨( )后(後)的( )空( )气(氣)显(顯)得( )特( )别(別)重( )。
    - Yǔ hòu de kōngqì xiǎnde tèbié zhòng.
    - The air feels especially heavy after the rain.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 重](@media/glyph91cd.png)
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 人
- **definition:** man

- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts side view of a standing man, highlighting an arm and a leg ->
    - visual representation of a person
  - **interpretation:** The character 人 (rén, 'person') effectively uses simple lines to depict the essential form of a human, making it immediately recognizable and intuitive as meaning 'person.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)好( )人( )。
    - Tā shì gè hǎo rén.
    - He is a good person.
  - 每( )个(個)人( )都( )有( )责(責)任( )，为(為)社( )会(會)作( )出( )贡(貢)献(獻)。
    - Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zérèn, wèi shèhuì zuòchū gòngxiàn.
    - Every person has a responsibility to contribute to society.
  - 人在旅途中，心( )灵(靈)获(獲)得( )了( )成( )长(長)。

### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 東
- **definition:** bag
- **pinyin:** dōng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bundle or bag tied at both ends
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a bundle or bag tied at both ends, it suggests convergence or collection, visually resembling a tied shape. This pictorial representation is historically connected to the direction 'east', as a bag captures and holds items, analogous to the rising sun being 'held' in the east. Despite alternative interpretations involving the sun and tree, the pictogram of a bundle remains the most direct visual link.
- **examples:**
  - 他的東里(裏)装(裝)满(滿)了( )书(書)。
    - Tā de dōng lǐ zhuāng mǎn le shū.
    - His bag is full of books.
  - 她( )买(買)了( )一( )个(個)新( )的( )東( )。
    - Tā mǎi le yī gè xīn de dōng.
    - She bought a new bag.
  - 在( )办(辦)公( )室( )里(裏)，他的東总(總)是( )放( )在( )椅( )子( )后(後)面( )。
    - Zài bàngōngshì lǐ, tā de dōng zǒng shì fàng zài yǐzi hòumiàn.
    - In the office, his bag is always placed behind the chair.
- **historical forms:**
