  - 亼
    - jí
    - mouth
  - 皀
    - jí
    - bowl of rice on a stand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 亼 (mouth) over 皀 (bowl of rice on a stand) ->
    - depicts a bowl with a stand
  - **interpretation:** The character 𠊊 visually represents a mouth above a bowl of rice on a stand, indicating a utensil or vessel used for holding food. This image directly links to the idea of a 'bowl with stand.' The component 皀 specifically conveys the idea of a container or bowl elevated, which situates its primary meaning in the context of serving or holding food.
### 食物 → 食 → 𠊊 → 皀
- **definition:** bowl of rice on a stand
- **pinyin:** jí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a bowl of rice on a stand ->
    - part of the radical 食 (shí, "food")
  - **interpretation:** The character 皀(bá, "acorn") depicts a bowl of rice on a stand, aligning it with food-related imagery.

### 食物 → 物
- **definition:** thing; object
- **pinyin:** wù
- **components:**
  - 牛
    - niú
    - cow
  - 勿
    - wù
    - do not
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 牛 (niú, "cow") + phonetic 勿 (wù) ->
    - thing; object
  - **interpretation:** The character 物 (wù, "thing; object") combines 牛 (niú, "cow") as the semantic component, signifying tangible entities or physical objects, with 勿 (wù) as the phonetic component to provide the sound. The usage of 牛, representing cattle, suggests that the meanings originally included tangible, concrete items before broadening to general objects.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)物( )品( )很( )贵(貴)重( )。
    - Zhège wùpǐn hěn guìzhòng.
    - This object is very valuable.
  - 我( )在( )寻(尋)找( )一( )个(個)特( )别(別)的( )东(東)西( )。
    - Wǒ zài xúnzhǎo yī gè tèbié de dōngxi.
    - I am looking for a special thing.
  - 每( )样(樣)物( )品( )都( )有( )其( )用( )途( )。
    - Měi yàng wùpǐn dōu yǒu qí yòngtú.
    - Every object has its use.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 物](@media/glyph7269.png)
### 食物 → 物 → 牛
- **definition:** cow
- **pinyin:** niú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the head and horns of an ox
  - **interpretation:** The character 牛 (niú, "ox") is a pictogram representing the head and horns of an ox, reflecting its importance in agriculture and daily life in ancient China.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )牵(牽)着(著)牛( )，去( )市( )场(場)买(買)东(東)西( )。
    - Nóngmín qiānzhe niú，qù shìchǎng mǎi dōngxī。
    - The farmer leads the ox and goes to the market to buy things.
  - 农(農)家( )养(養)牛( )，生活更加富足。
    - Nóngjiā yǎng niú，shēnghuó gèngjiā fùzú。
    - Rural families raise oxen, making their lives more prosperous.
  - 这(這)个(個)牛( )非( )常( )听(聽)话(話)。
    - Zhège niú fēicháng tīnghuà。
    - This ox is very obedient.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 牛](@media/glyph725b.png)
### 食物 → 物 → 勿
- **definition:** do not
- **pinyin:** wù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts blood on a knife ->
    - original character of 刎 (wěn, "to cut") ->
    - jiajie for 勿 (wù, "do not")
  - **interpretation:** The character 勿(wù), originally illustrated blood on a knife, visually represented the action of cutting, connecting to its initial meaning. Over time, it was borrowed to express prohibition or negation, showing the evolution beyond its pictorial roots.
- **examples:**
  - 进(進)入( )图(圖)书(書)馆(館)时(時)，请(請)勿( )大( )声(聲)喧( )哗(嘩)。
    - Jìnrù túshūguǎn shí, qǐng wù dàshēng xuānhuá.
    - When entering the library, please do not speak loudly.
  - 办(辦)公( )区(區)内(內)勿( )吸( )烟(煙)。
    - Bàngōng qū nèi wù xīyān.
    - Do not smoke in the office area.
  - 行( )路( )时(時)，勿( )追( )逐( )打( )闹(鬧)。
    - Xínglù shí, wù zhuīzhú dǎnào.
    - When walking, do not engage in chasing and rough play.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 勿](@media/glyph52ff.png)

## 充值卡

## recharge card
### chōngzhíkǎ
---
- **definition:** recharge card
- **pinyin:** chōngzhíkǎ
- **components:**
  - 充值
    - chōngzhí
    - to recharge; to top up
  - 卡
    - kǎ
    - card
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 充值 (chōngzhí, "to recharge") + 卡 (kǎ, "card") forms the word 充值卡, which refers to a card used for adding credit or value, typically to a prepaid account.
  - **interpretation:** 充值 (chōngzhí, "to recharge") combines 充 (chōng, "fill") with 值 (zhí, "value") to describe the action of adding value or balance to an account. 卡 (kǎ, "card") refers to a physical card, often used for storing or facilitating the transfer of this balance. Thus, a 充值卡 is a card used specifically for recharging or topping up the balance of an account, such as a prepaid phone plan.
- **examples:**
  - 你需要充值卡来(來)加( )值( )手( )机(機)话(話)费(費)。
    - Nǐ xūyào chōngzhíkǎ lái jiāzhí shǒujī huàfèi.
    - You need a recharge card to top up your mobile phone balance.
  - 他( )买(買)了( )一( )张(張)充( )值( )卡( )，准(準)备(備)给(給)游(遊)戏(戲)账(賬)号(號)充( )值( )。
    - Tā mǎile yī zhāng chōngzhíkǎ, zhǔnbèi gěi yóuxì zhànghào chōngzhí.
    - He bought a recharge card to top up his game account.
  - 在便利店，你( )可( )以( )很( )方( )便( )地( )购(購)买(買)充( )值( )卡( )。
    - Zài biànlìdiàn, nǐ kěyǐ hěn fāngbiàn de gòumǎi chōngzhíkǎ.
    - In the convenience store, you can easily purchase a recharge card.
### 充值卡 → 充值
- **definition:** to recharge; to top up
- **pinyin:** chōngzhí
- **components:**
  - 充
    - chōng
    - fill; full; satisfy
  - 值
    - zhí
    - value; worth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 充 (chōng, "fill; full; satisfy") originally suggests filling a space, like pouring liquid into a container until it is full. It extends metaphorically to the action of adding value to a balance, completing or satisfying a requirement. 值 (zhí, "value; worth") refers to the concept of worth or numerical value, especially in terms of money or morals. The combination of these characters forms the action of replenishing or increasing value, particularly in credit or prepaid accounts.
  - **interpretation:** 充值 portrays the act of filling (充) a monetary balance or value (值). 充 implies actively making something complete or reaching its full potential, while 值 denotes the aspect of monetary worth or available credit. When combined, it applies primarily to the function of adding credit to accounts such as phone balances or transportation cards, mirroring the act of filling them with usable value.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )需( )要( )充( )值( )我( )的( )手( )机(機)话(話)费(費)。
    - Wǒ xūyào chōngzhí wǒ de shǒujī huàfèi.
    - I need to recharge my phone credit.
  - 他在公交卡上充值，以( )便( )通( )勤( )时(時)使( )用( )。
    - Tā zài gōngjiāo kǎ shàng chōngzhí, yǐbiàn tōngqín shí shǐyòng.
    - He topped up his bus card so he can use it for commuting.
  - 为(為)了( )享( )受( )更( )多( )优(優)惠( )，会(會)员(員)们(們)可( )以( )充( )值( )账(賬)户(戶)。
    - Wèi le xiǎngshòu gèng duō yōuhuì, huìyuánmen kěyǐ chōngzhí zhànghù.
    - To enjoy more benefits, members can recharge their accounts.
### 充值卡 → 充值 → 充
- **definition:** fill; full; satisfy
- **pinyin:** chōng
- **components:**
  - 𠫓
    - nán
    - a newborn
  - 儿(兒)
    - ér
    - standing person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 𠫓 ('a newborn') + 儿 ('standing person') =
    - A newborn grows to stand and become mature ->
    - to fill; to act in place of
  - **interpretation:** The imagery of a newborn growing to eventually stand captures the idea of filling or becoming sufficient, as the development process implies providing what is needed or fulfilling potential. This extends to 'acting in place of', since a mature person can take on roles once dependent on others, emphasizing sufficiency and capability.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )需( )要( )给(給)我( )的( )手( )机(機)充( )电(電)。
    - Wǒ xūyào gěi wǒ de shǒujī chōngdiàn.
    - I need to charge my phone.
  - 厨(廚)房( )里(裡)的( )瓶( )子( )装(裝)满(滿)了( )水( )，充( )满(滿)了( )。
    - Chúfáng lǐ de píngzi zhuāng mǎn le shuǐ，chōngmǎn le.
    - The bottle in the kitchen is filled with water, it's full.
  - 在( )工( )作( )中( )，他( )常( )常( )充( )当(當)领(領)导(導)。
    - Zài gōngzuò zhōng, tā chángcháng chōngdāng lǐngdǎo.
    - At work, he often acts as the leader.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 充](@media/glyph5145.png)
### 充值卡 → 充值 → 充 → 𠫓
- **definition:** a newborn
- **pinyin:** nán
- **components:**
  - 母
    - mǔ
    - woman
  - 子
    - zǐ
    - child

  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 母 (mǔ, "woman") + 子 (zǐ, "child") =
    - a woman giving birth to a child ->
    - original form of 育
  - **interpretation:** The pictogram represents the concept of giving birth, with 母 symbolizing the mother and 子 the child, depicting the act of a woman giving birth. This directly leads to the meanings associated with birth and care of offspring.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 𠫓](@media/glyph20ad3.png)
### 充值卡 → 充值 → 充 → 𠫓 → 母
- **definition:** woman
- **pinyin:** mǔ
- **components:**
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - woman
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** differentiated form
  - **description:**
    - 女 (nǚ, "woman") + addition of two dots ->
    - distinguished as 母 (mǔ, "mother")
  - **interpretation:** The character 母 (mǔ, "mother") is a differentiated form of 女 (nǚ, "woman") with the addition of two dots to distinguish it. In early script forms, both 母 (mǔ, "mother") and 女 (nǚ, "woman") were represented by the same form as 女, necessitating the addition of dots to create a visual distinction. The character 毋, originally borrowed from 母, further specialized by changing the dots to a stroke to avoid confusion between the characters.
- **examples:**
  - 母( )给(給)了( )我( )无(無)限( )的( )温(溫)暖( )。
    - mǔ gěi le wǒ wúxiàn de wēnnuǎn
    - Mother has given me endless warmth.
  - 我( )的( )母( )温(溫)柔( )而( )坚(堅)毅( )，培( )养(養)了( )我( )良( )好( )的( )品( )格( )。
    - wǒ de mǔ wēnróu ér jiānyì, péiyǎng le wǒ liánghǎo de pǐngé
    - My mother is gentle and strong, nurturing my good character.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 母](@media/glyph6bcd.png)
### 充值卡 → 充值 → 充 → 𠫓 → 母 → 女
- **definition:** woman
- **pinyin:** nǚ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a woman kneeling or standing ->
    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )才( )华(華)横(橫)溢( )的( )女( )，赢(贏)得( )了( )全( )场(場)注( )目( )。
    - Nà wèi cáihuá héngyì de nǚ, yíng dé le quánchǎng zhùmù.
    - That talented woman won everyone's attention.
  - 古( )时(時)候( )‘女( )’常( )被( )用( )来(來)象( )征(徵)温(溫)柔( )，而( )如( )今( )它( )代( )表( )独(獨)立( )自( )信( )。
    - Gǔshíhòu ‘nǚ’ cháng bèi yòng lái xiàngzhēng wēnróu, ér rújīn tā dàibiǎo dúlì zìxìn.
    - In ancient times, the character '女' was often used to symbolize gentleness, whereas today it represents independence and confidence.

### 充值卡 → 充值 → 充 → 𠫓 → 子
- **definition:** child
- **pinyin:** zǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a baby with a large head and spread arms, with legs wrapped in a blanket
  - **interpretation:** The character 子(zǐ, "child") is a pictorial representation of a baby, emphasizing characteristics like a large head and outstretched arms typical of infants, which conveys the essence of a child. This visual element helps infer its meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)小( )子( )很( )调(調)皮( )。
    - Zhè xiǎo zǐ hěn tiáopí.
    - This kid is very naughty.
  - 他( )的( )小( )子( )爱(愛)打( )篮(籃)球( )，每( )天( )都( )锻(鍛)炼(煉)身( )体(體)。
    - Tā de xiǎo zǐ ài dǎ lánqiú, měitiān dōu duànliàn shēntǐ.
    - His child loves playing basketball, and practices every day.
  - 孤( )独(獨)的( )子( )需( )要( )关(關)爱(愛)。
    - Gūdú de zǐ xūyào guān'ài.
    - A lonely child needs care.

### 充值卡 → 充值 → 充 → 儿(兒)
- **traditional:** 兒
- **definition:** standing person
- **pinyin:** ér
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an infant with an imperfect cranium (fontanelle)
  - **interpretation:** The character 兒 (ér, "child") is a pictogram representing a child with a distinctive fontanelle, highlighting the child's young age and the characteristic soft spot on the head. This reflects the character's meaning as 'child.'
  - **simplification:** 儿 is a simplified and cursive form of 兒, representing a stylistic reduction of strokes while still retaining the essence of a child's figure.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)小( )女( )孩( )喜( )欢(歡)唱( )歌( )。
    - Zhè gè xiǎo nǚhái xǐhuān chànggē.
    - This little girl likes to sing.
  - 他( )有( )一( )个(個)聪(聰)明( )的( )儿(兒)子( )。
    - Tā yǒu yī gè cōngmíng de érzi.
    - He has a smart son.
  - 每( )个(個)父( )母( )都( )希( )望( )自( )己( )的( )孩( )子( )健( )康( )快( )乐(樂)。

It visually represents a standing person, maintaining the core meaning of 'person' while facilitating the construction of complex characters.

## 无(無)法( )
---
## unable
### wúfǎ
---
- **traditional:** 無法
- **definition:** unable
- **pinyin:** wúfǎ
- **components:**
  - 无(無)
    - wú
    - without; none; lack
  - 法
    - fǎ
    - method; law; way
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** These two characters together express the concept of being unable or without means.
  - **interpretation:** 無(wú, "without; none; lack") indicates nonexistence or the absence of something, often setting the stage for an impossibility. 法(fǎ, "method; law; way") refers to a manner, method, or means by which something can be done. Together, 無法(wúfǎ, "unable") suggests a lack of means or method, thus expressing the idea of being unable to do something.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)无(無)法( )抵( )达(達)那( )里(裡)。
    - Wǒmen wúfǎ dǐdá nàlǐ.
    - We are unable to reach there.
  - 他( )因( )受( )伤(傷)而( )无(無)法( )比( )赛(賽)。
    - Tā yīn shòushāng ér wúfǎ bǐsài.
    - He is unable to compete due to injury.
  - 这(這)个(個)问(問)题(題)太( )复(複)杂(雜)以( )至( )于(於)我( )们(們)无(無)法( )解( )决(決)。
    - Zhège wèntí tài fùzá yǐ zhìyú wǒmen wúfǎ jiějué.
    - This problem is too complex for us to solve.
### 无(無)法( ) → 无(無)
- **traditional:** 無
- **definition:** without; none; lack
- **pinyin:** wú
- **components:**
  - 元
    - yuán
    - origin
  - 天
    - tiān
    - heaven
  - 堯
    - yáo
    - a legendary emperor; tall
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** unknown
  - **description:** Unknown origin: possibly related to 元 (yuán, 'origin'), 天 (tiān, 'heaven'), or 堯 (yáo, 'a legendary emperor; tall')
  - **interpretation:** The character's origin remains undetermined, but it shares usage with 無 (wú, 'not have; without') and was historically used interchangeably with it to represent the concept of non-existence or absence.
  - **simplification:** Simplified to 无 by streamlining strokes and adopting an alternate simple form for ease of writing and recognition.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )无(無)话(話)可( )说(說)。
    - Tā wú huà kě shuō.
    - He has nothing to say.
  - 城( )市( )的( )夜( )空( )几(幾)乎( )无(無)星( )，只有月亮。
    - Chéngshì de yèkōng jīhū wú xīng, zhǐ yǒu yuèliàng.
    - The city's night sky is almost without stars, only the moon.
  - 他( )们(們)之( )间(間)无(無)解( )，只( )能( )随(隨)缘(緣)。
    - Tāmen zhījiān wú jiě, zhǐ néng suíyuán.
    - There's no solution between them, they can only go with the flow.

- **examples:**
  - 万(萬)物( )之( )元( )来(來)自( )于(於)混( )沌( )。
    - Wànwù zhī yuán láizì yú húndùn.
    - The origin of all things comes from chaos.
  - 道( )家( )的( )理( )念( )追( )寻(尋)心( )灵(靈)的( )元( )。
    - Dàojiā de lǐniàn zhuīxún xīnlíng de yuán.

### 无(無)法( ) → 无(無) → 天
- **definition:** heaven
- **pinyin:** tiān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Horizontal line above 大(dà, "big") to depict sky ->
    - anthropomorphic man with large head symbolizing heaven
  - **interpretation:** The character 天(tiān) originally depicts the sky as a horizontal line above a person with outstretched arms, 大(dà, "big"), emphasizing the vastness or domain of heaven. Over time, the character evolved into an anthropomorphic representation of heaven, which later influenced its interpretation in classical texts.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)，天( )边(邊)星( )光( )闪(閃)烁(爍)。
    - Yèmù jiànglín, tiānbiān xīnguāng shǎnshuò.
    - As night falls, starlight gleams on the horizon.
  - 他仰望高空，祈( )求( )天( )佑( )众(眾)生( )。
    - Tā yǎngwàng gāokōng, qíqiú tiānyòu zhòngshēng.
    - He gazes up to the sky and prays for heaven's protection over all living beings.

### 无(無)法( ) → 无(無) → 堯
- **definition:** a legendary emperor; tall
- **pinyin:** yáo
- **components:**
  - 垚
    - yáo
    - tall (of earth)
  - 兀
    - wù
    - towering and bald
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound and ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic 垚 (earth layers, *ŋeːw) + semantic 兀 (towering and bald) ->
    - Depicts tall earth + kneeling man =
    - legendary ruler
  - **interpretation:** The character 堯 (yáo) combines phonetic 垚, which represents layers of earth and sounds like *ŋeːw, with semantic 兀, depicting a towering and bald figure. This combination suggests a legendary ruler who stands tall like the earth and exhibits a commanding presence. Such a ruler is perceived as lofty and powerful, akin to the towering formation of earth.

    - "tall (of earth)"

- **examples:**

### 无(無)法( ) → 无(無) → 堯 → 兀

  - **description:** 兀 is a variant form of 尢 (wān, "man with bent legs")

    - Gǔ shù zhīgàn wùrán luǒlù, sùshuōzhe suìyuè cāngsāng.

### 无(無)法( ) → 法
- **definition:** method; law; way
- **pinyin:** fǎ
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 去
    - qù
    - go
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 去 (qù, "go") ->
    - method
  - **interpretation:** The character 法(fǎ, "method; law") combines the semantic component 氵 (shuǐ, "water") suggesting flow or enactment with the phonetic component 去 (qù, "go"), which provides the sound. The concept of water implies a guiding principle or natural flow, which together with the action of 'going', forms the idea of following a prescribed path or method, hence 'law' or 'method'.
  - **simplification:** Originally simplified from 灋, resulting in 法 by retaining key phonetic and semantic elements and omitting structurally non-essential components.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)方( )法( )很( )有( )效( )。
    - Zhège fāngfǎ hěn yǒuxiào.
    - This method is very effective.
  - 学(學)习(習)法( )律( )是( )一( )个(個)挑( )战(戰)。
    - Xuéxí fǎlǜ shì yī gè tiǎozhàn.
    - Studying law is a challenge.
  - 我( )们(們)需( )要( )找( )到( )更( )好( )的( )解( )决(決)办(辦)法( )。
    - Wǒmen xūyào zhǎodào gèng hǎo de jiějué bànfǎ.
    - We need to find a better solution.

### 无(無)法( ) → 法 → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 无(無)法( ) → 法 → 去
- **definition:** go
- **pinyin:** qù
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth, object
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 大 (dà, "big") + 口 (kǒu, "mouth, object") =
    - originally depicted a man departing from a cave or city ->
    - meaning "go"
  - **interpretation:** The original form illustrated a man leaving a place, possibly a city or a cave, suggesting the concept of going away or departing. This imagery naturally leads to the modern meaning of "go."
- **examples:**
  - 我( )要( )去( )学(學)校( )。
    - Wǒ yào qù xuéxiào.
    - I want to go to school.
  - 她去商店，买(買)了( )一( )些( )水( )果( )。
    - Tā qù shāngdiàn, mǎile yīxiē shuǐguǒ.
    - She went to the shop and bought some fruits.
  - 明( )年( )我( )打( )算( )去( )法( )国(國)。
    - Míngnián wǒ dǎsuàn qù Fǎguó.
    - I plan to go to France next year.
- **historical forms:**

### 无(無)法( ) → 法 → 去 → 大
- **definition:** big
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 大](@media/glyph5927.png)
### 无(無)法( ) → 法 → 去 → 大 → 夫
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 夫](@media/glyph592b.png)
### 无(無)法( ) → 法 → 去 → 口
- **definition:** mouth, object
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.

## 一切

## everything
### yīqiè
---
- **definition:** everything
- **pinyin:** yīqiè
- **components:**
  - 一
    - yī
    - one; single; entire
  - 切
    - qiè
    - all; each; every; to cut
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** The word 一切 (yīqiè, "everything") is composed of two characters: 一 (yī, "one") and 切 (qiè, "all; each").
  - **interpretation:** The character 一 (yī, "one") signifies singularity and entirety, suggesting totality when combined with 切 (qiè, "all; each"), which extends the concept to mean 'collectively' or 'every single one.' Together, 一切 (yīqiè) embodies the idea of 'everything' by emphasizing the inclusion of all possible parts or elements as a single unified whole.
- **examples:**
  - 一( )切( )都( )会(會)好( )起( )来(來)。
    - Yīqiè dōu huì hǎo qǐlái.
    - Everything will get better.
  - 我( )们(們)需( )要( )为(為)一( )切( )做( )好( )准(準)备(備)。
    - Wǒmen xūyào wèi yīqiè zuò hǎo zhǔnbèi.
    - We need to prepare for everything.
  - 他( )失( )去( )了( )对(對)一( )切( )的( )信( )心( )。
    - Tā shīqùle duì yīqiè de xìnxīn.
    - He lost confidence in everything.
### 一切 → 一
- **definition:** one; single; entire
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.
    - He only saw one bird, which flew into the garden.
  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.
    - One straight road leads to the distance.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 一](@media/glyph4e00.png)
### 一切 → 切
- **definition:** all; each; every; to cut
- **pinyin:** qiè
- **components:**
  - 七
    - qī
    - seven
  - 刀
    - dāo
    - knife
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 七 (qī, 'seven') + Semantic: 刀 (dāo, 'knife') =
    - sharp object ->
    - to cut
  - **interpretation:** The character 切(qiè, 'to cut') combines the sound component 七(qī, 'seven') with the semantic component 刀(dāo, 'knife'). This implies the action of cutting with a sharp tool.
- **examples:**
  - 厨(廚)房( )的( )刀( )非( )常( )锋(鋒)利( )，切( )菜( )时(時)请(請)小( )心( )。
    - chúfáng de dāo fēicháng fēnglì, qiè cài shí qǐng xiǎoxīn.
    - The kitchen knife is very sharp, so be careful when cutting vegetables.
  - 一( )切( )问(問)题(題)都( )能( )解( )决(決)。
    - yīqiè wèntí dōu néng jiějué.
    - Every problem can be solved.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 切](@media/glyph5207.png)
### 一切 → 切 → 七
- **definition:** seven
- **pinyin:** qī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a vertical stroke intersecting a horizontal stroke
  - **interpretation:** The character 七(qī, 'seven') is a pictogram that visually represents a structure formed by intersecting strokes. Its form does not directly convey numerical value but was historically used because of its visual structure, which resembles cuts or divisions.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )有( )七( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Tā yǒu qī gè píngguǒ.
    - He has seven apples.
  - 今天是七月七日。
    - Jīntiān shì qī yuè qī rì.
    - Today is July 7th.
  - 我( )们(們)七( )点(點)钟(鐘)见(見)面( )。
    - Wǒmen qī diǎn zhōng jiànmiàn.
    - We'll meet at seven o'clock.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 七](@media/glyph4e03.png)

- **definition:** knife
- **pinyin:** dāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a knife
  - **interpretation:** 刀 (dāo, "knife") is a pictographic representation of a knife or blade, with a sharp edge and a handle, resembling the form of a basic cutting tool.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )锋(鋒)利( )的( )刀( )，切( )开(開)蔬( )菜( )。
    - tā yòng fēnglì de dāo, qiē kāi shūcài.
    - He uses a sharp knife to cut vegetables.
  - 这(這)把( )刀( )非( )常( )锋(鋒)利( )。
    - zhè bǎ dāo fēicháng fēnglì.
    - This knife is very sharp.
  - 厨(廚)师(師)小( )心( )地( )持( )刀( )，为(為)客( )人( )切( )肉( )。
    - chúshī xiǎoxīn de chí dāo, wèi kèrén qiē ròu.
    - The chef holds the knife carefully and slices meat for the guest.

## 帮(幫)助( )
---
## help
### bāngzhù
---
- **traditional:** 幫助
- **definition:** help
- **pinyin:** bāngzhù
- **components:**
  - 帮(幫)
    - bāng
    - help; assist; group; gang
  - 助
    - zhù
    - help; aid; assist; support
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 帮(幫) (bāng, "help") refers to aiding or assisting someone, often in a collaborative sense. 助 (zhù, "help") emphasizes the act of giving aid or support.
  - **interpretation:** 帮(幫) (bāng, "help") conveys the idea of a group working together, which naturally extends to providing assistance or aid. 助 (zhù, "help") involves offering active support or assistance, emphasizing the act of augmenting another's efforts. Together, 帮(幫)助( ) (bāngzhù) combines the collaborative aspect with active support, creating a comprehensive notion of helping.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )总(總)是( )愿(願)意( )帮(幫)助( )别(別)人( )。
    - Tā zǒngshì yuànyì bāngzhù biérén.
    - He is always willing to help others.
  - 谢(謝)谢(謝)你( )的( )帮(幫)助( )，我非常感激。
    - Xièxiè nǐ de bāngzhù, wǒ fēicháng gǎnjī.
    - Thank you for your help, I am very grateful.
  - 他( )需( )要( )一( )些( )帮(幫)助( )。
    - Tā xūyào yīxiē bāngzhù.
    - He needs some help.
### 帮(幫)助( ) → 帮(幫)
- **traditional:** 幫
- **definition:** help; assist; group; gang
- **pinyin:** bāng
- **components:**
  - 巾
    - jīn
    - cloth
  - 邦
    - bāng
    - country
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 巾 (jīn, "cloth") + phonetic: 邦 (bāng, "country") =
    - assisting with cloth or materials ->
    - to help
  - **interpretation:** The character 幫 combines the meaning of 巾 (jīn, "cloth") with the phonetic element 邦 (bāng, "country"), symbolizing assistance or help with physical materials like cloth. This reflects the role of providing tangible support.
  - **simplification:** In the simplified form 帮, 巾 (jīn, "cloth") is retained while 邦 (bāng, "country") is kept intact from the traditional representation, aligning with historical simplification practices from 1956.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )来(來)帮(幫)你( )。
    - Wǒ lái bāng nǐ.
    - I will help you.
  - 他( )帮(幫)老( )师(師)，辅(輔)导(導)学(學)生( )。
    - Tā bāng lǎoshī, fǔdǎo xuésheng.
    - He helps the teacher, tutoring the students.
  - 遇( )到( )困( )难(難)时(時)，朋( )友( )会(會)帮(幫)忙( )。
    - Yùdào kùnnán shí, péngyǒu huì bāngmáng.
    - When encountering difficulties, friends will help.
### 帮(幫)助( ) → 帮(幫) → 巾
- **definition:** cloth
- **pinyin:** jīn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a piece of cloth with two ends hanging down
  - **interpretation:** The character 巾(jīn, "cloth") visually represents a cloth or towel, with its depiction emphasizing the idea of a piece of fabric and its functionality.
- **examples:**
  - 爸( )爸( )用( )干(乾)净(淨)的( )巾( )擦( )手( )。
    - Bàba yòng gānjìng de jīn cā shǒu.
    - Dad uses a clean cloth to wipe his hands.
  - 她( )拿( )着(著)一( )条(條)巾( )，轻(輕)轻(輕)擦( )拭( )眼( )角( )。
    - Tā názhe yì tiáo jīn, qīngqīng cā shì yǎnjiǎo.
    - She holds a cloth and gently wipes the corners of her eyes.
  - 老( )人( )用( )巾( )给(給)手( )上( )包( )扎( )伤(傷)口( )。
    - Lǎorén yòng jīn gěi shǒu shàng bāozā shāngkǒu.
    - The elderly person uses a cloth to bandage the wound on his hand.

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 帮(幫) → 邦
- **definition:** country
- **pinyin:** bāng
- **components:**
  - 丰
    - fēng
    - abundant
  - 阝
    - fù
    - earthen mound
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 丰 (fēng, "abundant") + Semantic: 阝 (fù, "earthen mound") =
    - representation of an earthen mound + abundant resources ->
    - nation
  - **interpretation:** The character 邦(bāng, "nation") combines the idea of wealth or resources suggested by 丰 with the earthen boundary suggested by 阝, symbolizing a defined area with resources, which is the concept of a nation.
- **examples:**
  - 历(歷)史( )塑( )造( )了( )这(這)个(個)邦( )，使( )之( )繁( )荣(榮)昌( )盛( )。
    - Lìshǐ sùzào le zhège bāng, shǐ zhī fánróng chāngshèng。
    - History shaped this nation, making it prosperous.
  - 一( )个(個)善( )良( )的( )邦( )能( )让(讓)人( )民( )安( )居( )乐(樂)业(業)。
    - Yīgè shànliáng de bāng néng ràng rénmín ānjū lèyè。
    - A good nation enables its people to live in peace and prosperity.

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 帮(幫) → 邦 → 丰(豐)
- **traditional:** 豐
- **definition:** abundant
- **pinyin:** fēng
- **components:**
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - ground
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a luxuriant plant growing on the ground (土) ->
    - modern version simplified, missing bottom stroke ->
    - similar to top component of 豐 (fēng, 'abundant') and 奉 (fèng, 'offer')
  - **interpretation:** The character 丰(豐) (fēng, 'abundant') originated as a pictogram depicting a plant thriving on the ground, symbolizing abundance. Over time, the detailed strokes representing the soil and growth were removed, simplifying the character.
- **examples:**
  - 今年的收成很丰(豐)。
    - Jīnnián de shōuchéng hěn fēng.
    - This year's harvest is very abundant.

    - Tiánlǐ de zhuāngjià zhǎng de shífēn fēng, nóngmínmen xiào zhú yánkāi.
    - The crops in the field grew very abundantly, and the farmers beamed with joy.
  - 菜园里的番茄结得丰(豐)硕(碩)，老( )爷(爺)爷(爺)摘( )得( )满(滿)满(滿)当(當)当(當)。
    - Càiyuán lǐ de fānqié jié de fēngshuò, lǎo yéyé zhāi de mǎnmǎndāngdāng.
    - The tomatoes in the vegetable garden bore abundant fruit, and the old man picked them in plenty.

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 帮(幫) → 邦 → 丰(豐) → 土

- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 帮(幫) → 邦 → 阝

  - **interpretation:** The character 阝 (fù, 'mound') visually represents a small elevation of land, resembling a hillside or embankment.
- **examples:**
  - 小( )山( )顶(頂)上( )耸(聳)立( )着(著)阝(阜)。
    - Xiǎo shāndǐng shàng sǒnglì zhe fù.

  - 山( )坡( )上( )长(長)满(滿)了( )绿(綠)色( )的( )草( )丛(叢)，山( )丘( )边(邊)坐( )落( )着(著)一( )座( )古( )老( )的( )阝(阜)。
    - Shānpō shàng cháng mǎn le lǜsè de cǎocóng, shānqiū biān zuòluò zhe yī zuò gǔlǎo de fù.

  - 远(遠)处(處)沙( )丘( )边(邊)隐(隱)约(約)可( )见(見)一( )堆( )阝(阜)，仿(彷)佛(彿)历(歷)史( )的( )见(見)证(證)。

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 助
- **definition:** help; aid; assist; support
- **pinyin:** zhù
- **components:**
  - 力
    - lì
    - strong arm
  - 且
    - qiě
    - moreover
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 力 (力) (lì, "strong arm") + phonetic 且 (且) (qiě) ->
    - to help with one’s strength
  - **interpretation:** The character 助 (zhù, "aid") combines 力 (力) (lì, "strong arm") as the semantic element, suggesting the use of strength, with 且 (且) (qiě) as the phonetic element providing the sound. The concept links physical strength to the act of assisting, thus forming the meaning of 'aid' or 'support.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )总(總)是( )乐(樂)于(於)助( )人( )。
    - Tā zǒng shì lè yú zhù rén.
    - He is always willing to help others.
  - 需( )要( )帮(幫)助( )的( )时(時)候( )，可( )以( )随(隨)时(時)找( )我( )。
    - Xūyào bāngzhù de shíhòu, kěyǐ suíshí zhǎo wǒ.
    - When you need help, you can reach out to me anytime.
  - 他( )的( )成( )功( )离(離)不( )开(開)家( )人( )的( )支( )持( )和( )帮(幫)助( )。

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 助 → 力
- **definition:** strong arm
- **pinyin:** lì
- **etymology:**

- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )尽(盡)全( )力( )，赢(贏)得( )比( )赛(賽)。
    - Tā yòng jìn quánlì, yíng dé bǐsài.
    - He used all his strength to win the competition.
  - 他( )把( )力( )气(氣)用( )在( )学(學)习(習)上( )。
    - Tā bǎ lìqì yòng zài xuéxí shàng.
    - He applies his strength to his studies.
  - 面( )对(對)困( )难(難)，他( )毫( )不( )退( )缩(縮)，全力以赴。
    - Miànduì kùnnán, tā háo bù tuìsuō, quánlì yǐ fù.

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 助 → 且
- **definition:** moreover
- **pinyin:** qiě
- **components:**
  - 祖
    - zǔ
    - ancestor
  - 俎
    - zǔ
    - sacrificial vessel
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a stone altar or a sacrificial vessel from above ->
    - original form of 俎 (zǔ, "sacrificial vessel") ->
    - borrowed for ancestor rites in 祖 (zǔ, "ancestor")
  - **interpretation:** The character 且(qiě) originally depicted a stone altar used in sacrificial rites or a vessel for holding meat, reflecting its association with mealtime offerings or ancestor worship in characters like 祖(zǔ, "ancestor").
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)家( )餐( )厅(廳)的( )食( )物( )美( )味( )，且( )服( )务(務)一( )流( )。
    - Zhè jiā cāntīng de shíwù měiwèi, qiě fúwù yīliú.
    - The food at this restaurant is delicious, and furthermore, the service is excellent.
  - 他( )不( )仅(僅)聪(聰)明( )，且待人友善。
    - Tā bùjǐn cōngmíng, qiě dàirén yǒushàn.
    - He is not only smart, but also treats people kindly.
  - 我( )们(們)需( )努( )力( )工( )作( )，且( )保( )持( )乐(樂)观(觀)态(態)度( )。
    - Wǒmen xū nǔlì gōngzuò, qiě bǎochí lèguān tàidù.

### 帮(幫)助( ) → 助 → 且 → 祖
- **definition:** ancestor
- **pinyin:** zǔ
- **components:**
  - 示
    - shì
    - to show; indicate
  - 且
    - qiě
    - moreover; furthermore
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 示 (shì, "to show; indicate") + phonetic 且 (qiě) ->
    - ancestor
  - **interpretation:** The component 示 (shì, "to show; indicate") is related to rituals and offering to ancestors, symbolizing the spiritual connection to them. The phonetic component 且 (qiě) contributes the sound and also hints at the idea of continuation through generations, aligning with the concept of 'ancestor' as a source from which things originate.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)的( )祖( )先( )在( )这(這)里(裡)生( )活( )了( )很( )多( )年( )。
    - Wǒmen de zǔxiān zài zhèlǐ shēnghuóle hěn duō nián.
    - Our ancestors lived here for many years.
  - 过(過)年( )的( )时(時)候( )，我( )们(們)会(會)祭( )拜( )祖( )先( )。
    - Guònián de shíhòu, wǒmen huì jì bài zǔxiān.
    - During the New Year, we will pay homage to our ancestors.

    - additional strokes added to distinguish from unrelated characters

  - 古( )人( )以( )卜( )示( )未( )来(來)。

## happy
### kāixīn
---
- **traditional:** 開心
- **definition:** happy
- **pinyin:** kāixīn
- **components:**
  - 开(開)
    - kāi
    - open; start
  - 心
    - xīn
    - heart; mind
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 開(开)心( ) comprises 開(开) (kāi, "open") and 心 (xīn, "heart"). The combination suggests opening one's heart, allowing happiness and joy to enter.
  - **interpretation:** The term 開(开)心( ) evokes the image of opening one's heart to let in happiness and positive emotions. It is a vivid way of expressing the state of feeling happy or joyous.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )今( )天( )感( )觉(覺)很( )开(開)心( )。
    - Wǒ jīntiān gǎnjué hěn kāixīn.
    - I feel happy today.
  - 他( )听(聽)到( )好( )消( )息( )，变(變)得( )异(異)常( )开(開)心( )。
    - Tā tīngdào hǎo xiāoxī, biàn de yìcháng kāixīn.
    - He became extremely happy after hearing the good news.
  - 朋( )友( )聚( )会(會)时(時)，大( )家( )都( )很( )开(開)心( )。
    - Péngyǒu jùhuì shí, dàjiā dōu hěn kāixīn.
    - Everyone is happy during the friends' gathering.
### 开(開)心( ) → 开(開)
- **traditional:** 開
- **definition:** open; start
- **pinyin:** kāi
- **components:**
  - 門
    - mén
    - door
  - 一
    - yī
    - one; latch
  - 廾
    - gǒng
    - two hands
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 門 (mén, "door") + 一 (latch/pole) + 廾 (gǒng, "two hands") ->
    - hands opening a latched door ->
    - 開
  - **interpretation:** The character 開 (kāi, "open") visually represents the action of opening a door with hands, symbolized by combining the door 門 with the latch 一 and two hands 廾. This conveys the core idea of opening something secured by a latch.
  - **simplification:** 开 is a visual and structural shorthand of 開, created by merging 一 and 廾 into a simplified form, while retaining the reference to opening a door.
- **examples:**
  - 老( )师(師)在( )上( )课(課)时(時)总(總)是( )很( )开(開)心( )。
    - Lǎoshī zài shàngkè shí zǒng shì hěn kāixīn.
    - The teacher is always happy during class.
  - 我( )们(們)要( )早( )上( )开(開)会(會)，讨(討)论(論)计(計)划(劃)。
    - Wǒmen yào zǎoshang kāihuì, tǎolùn jìhuà.
    - We need to have a meeting in the morning to discuss the plan.
  - 请(請)把( )门(門)打( )开(開)，让(讓)我( )进(進)去( )。
    - Qǐng bǎ mén dǎkāi, ràng wǒ jìnqù.
    - Please open the door and let me in.
### 开(開)心( ) → 开(開) → 門
- **definition:** door
- **pinyin:** mén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a double-door gate
  - **interpretation:** The character depicts a pair of doors, visually representing the concept of a door or gate, which is fundamental in indicating entrance and exit points in structures.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )走( )到( )门(門)口( )。
    - Wǒ zǒu dào ménkǒu.
    - I walk to the door.
  - 门(門)轻(輕)轻(輕)打( )开(開)，房( )客( )进(進)来(來)了( )。
    - Mén qīngqīng dǎkāi, fángkè jìn láile.
    - The door opened gently, and the tenant came in.
- **historical forms:**

### 开(開)心( ) → 开(開) → 一
- **definition:** one; latch
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.

  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.

### 开(開)心( ) → 开(開) → 廾 → 𠂇 → 又
- **definition:** again
- **pinyin:** yòu

    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。

Over time, it has become highly stylized, but it continues to convey the notion of the heart as the center of emotions and consciousness.
- **examples:**

## 没(沒)事( )
---
## be free; no problem
### méishì
---
- **traditional:** 沒事
- **definition:** be free; no problem
- **pinyin:** méishì
- **components:**
  - 没(沒)
    - méi
    - not have; drown
  - 事
    - shì
    - matter; affair; event; task
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 沒 (méi, "not have; drown") + 事 (shì, "matter; affair") =
    - "no matter" ->
    - be free; no problem
  - **interpretation:** 沒 (méi, "not have") negates the presence or occurrence of something, while 事 (shì, "matter; affair") broadly encompasses events, issues, or tasks. Together, the term 沒事 (méishì, "be free; no problem") logically suggests that there is nothing on hand that warrants concern or attention. In essence, if there is no matter to deal with, one is free or unoccupied, and there are no complications.
- **examples:**
  - 我今天沒事。
    - Wǒ jīntiān méishì.
    - I'm free today.
  - 小問題，沒事。
    - Xiǎo wèntí, méishì.
    - It's a small issue, no problem.
  - 如果你沒事，我們可以一起去看電影。
    - Rúguǒ nǐ méishì, wǒmen kěyǐ yīqǐ qù kàn diànyǐng.
    - If you're free, we can go watch a movie together.
### 没(沒)事( ) → 没(沒)
- **traditional:** 沒
- **definition:** not have; drown
- **pinyin:** méi
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 殳
    - shū
    - an ancient weapon
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic: 殳 (shū) =
    - action related to water sinking or disappearing ->
    - final meaning of drowning/sinking or 'not have'
  - **interpretation:** The character 没(沒) (méi, "not have; without; drown; sink") combines the semantic element 氵 (shuǐ, "water") with the phonetic 殳 (shū) to associate the action of submerging or becoming imperceptible with water, reflecting the sense of losing something or lacking.
  - **simplification:** Simplified from 没(沒) (méi, "not have") by retaining original components, with no significant change in structure.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )没(沒)钱(錢)。
    - Wǒ méi qián.
    - I don't have money.
  - 他( )没(沒)看( )到( )信( )，就( )离(離)开(開)了( )。
    - Tā méi kàn dào xìn, jiù lí kāi le.
    - He didn't see the letter, and then he left.
  - 花( )园(園)里( )没(沒)人( )。
    - Huāyuán lǐ méi rén.
    - There is no one in the garden.
### 没(沒)事( ) → 没(沒) → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.

    - depicts hand holding a tool ->
    - borrowed meaning of bamboo halberd
  - **interpretation:** The character originally depicted a hand holding a tool, suggesting the action of beating. It was later borrowed to refer to a specific type of weapon, a bamboo halberd.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )代( )战(戰)场(場)上( )，殳( )曾( )被( )视(視)为(為)关(關)键(鍵)武( )器( )。
    - Gǔdài zhànchǎng shàng, shū céng bèi shì wéi guānjiàn wǔqì.
    - On ancient battlefields, the shū was once considered a crucial weapon.
  - 兵( )器( )展( )览(覽)中( )，殳( )闪(閃)耀( )着(著)历(歷)史( )的( )光( )芒( )。
    - Bīngqì zhǎnlǎn zhōng, shū shǎnyào zhe lìshǐ de guāngmáng.
    - In the arms exhibition, the shū shone with the brilliance of history.

- **definition:** matter; affair; event; task

  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 又 (yòu, "hand") + 中 (zhōng, "a flagpole with a drum") =
    - flag in hand ->
    - to perform one's job
  - **interpretation:** The character 事 (shì, "matter") represents the concept of handling or managing affairs, symbolized by holding a flag, indicating a formal duty or role. The flagpole with a drum suggests organized activity or official matters.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)件( )事( )很( )复(複)杂(雜)。
    - Zhè jiàn shì hěn fùzá.
    - This matter is very complicated.
  - 小( )事( )也( )能( )引( )发(發)大( )问(問)题(題)，我( )们(們)需( )要( )认(認)真( )对(對)待( )。
    - Xiǎo shì yě néng yǐn fā dà wèntí, wǒmen xūyào rènzhēn duìdài.
    - Even small matters can trigger big problems; we need to handle them seriously.
  - 他( )总(總)是( )处(處)理( )各( )种(種)事( )，不管大小。
    - Tā zǒng shì chǔlǐ gèzhǒng shì, bùguǎn dàxiǎo.
    - He always manages various affairs, regardless of their significance.
- **historical forms:**

### 没(沒)事( ) → 事 → 又
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** yòu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a right hand
  - **interpretation:** The character 又 (yòu, "hand") is a pictogram representing a right hand, showing the fingers and palm, which highlights its original meaning related to manual actions.
- **examples:**
  - 「又( )」这(這)个(個)字( )最( )早( )来(來)源( )于(於)手( )。
    - 「Yòu」 zhè gè zì zuì zǎo láiyuán yú shǒu。
    - The character '又' originally comes from the hand.
  - 很( )多( )汉(漢)字( )含( )有( )又( )部( )首( )，表( )示( )与(與)手( )有( )关(關)的( )动(動)作( )。
    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。
    - Gǔrén yòng Yòu miáohuì shǒu de xíngtài, tǐxiàn le gǔ hànzì de dútè mèilì。
    - Ancient people used '又' to depict the shape of the hand, showcasing the unique charm of ancient Chinese characters.

  - **description:** Depicts an arrow piercing through the center of a target
  - **interpretation:** The character 中 (zhōng, "middle") visually represents an arrow passing through the central point of a target, illustrating the concept of 'center' or 'middle' effectively. This visual imagery is straightforward and memorable, corresponding closely with its meaning of being at the center or middle of something.
- **examples:**
  - 他站在道路中，看( )着(著)远(遠)方( )。

## 人( )民( )日( )报(報)

## People’s Daily
### Rénmín Rìbào
---
- **traditional:** 人民日報
- **definition:** People’s Daily
- **pinyin:** Rénmín Rìbào
- **components:**
  - 人民
    - rénmín
    - people; citizens
  - 日( )报(報)
    - rìbào
    - daily newspaper
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 人民 (rénmín, "people"; "citizens") + 日報 (rìbào, "daily newspaper") =
    - People’s Daily, a newspaper meant for the general public
  - **interpretation:** 人民 (rénmín, "people"; "citizens") combines 人 (rén, "person") to emphasize individual members of society and 民 (mín, "people") referring to the masses, creating the collective idea of a populace or nation. 日報 (rìbào, "daily newspaper") combines 日 (rì, "day") indicating a daily occurrence and 報 (bào, "report"; "newspaper") to signify a daily publication of news and reports.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )每( )天( )都( )读(讀)人( )民( )日( )报(報)。
    - Wǒ měitiān dōu dú Rénmín Rìbào.
    - I read the People’s Daily every day.
  - 人( )民( )日( )报(報)报(報)道(導)了( )这(這)条(條)重( )要( )新( )闻(聞)。
    - Rénmín Rìbào bàodào le zhè tiáo zhòngyào xīnwén.
    - The People’s Daily reported this important news.
  - 他( )在( )人( )民( )日( )报(報)工( )作( )已( )有( )十( )年( )。
    - Tā zài Rénmín Rìbào gōngzuò yǐ yǒu shí nián.
    - He has been working at the People’s Daily for ten years.
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 人民
- **definition:** people; citizens
- **pinyin:** rénmín
- **components:**
  - 人
    - rén
    - person; human; individual; people
  - 民
    - mín
    - people; populace; citizenry; folk
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 人 (rén, "person") denotes an individual or human being. 民 (mín, "people") signifies the people or populace. Together, 人民 (rénmín, "the people") represents the collective body of individuals within a community or nation.
  - **interpretation:** 人 (rén, "person") signifies an individual human, while 民 (mín, "people") refers to the collective populace. The combination of these characters captures the concept of all individuals forming a nation or society. The image of many 人 (rén, "person") constituting a single 民 (mín, "people") group effectively conveys the idea of a community or population as a whole.
- **examples:**
  - 人民是( )国(國)家( )的( )基( )础(礎)。
    - Rénmín shì guójiā de jīchǔ.
    - The people are the foundation of the nation.
  - 政( )府( )应(應)该(該)服( )务(務)人( )民( )，不是官僚。
    - Zhèngfǔ yīnggāi fúwù rénmín, bùshì guānliáo.
    - The government should serve the people, not bureaucrats.
  - 人( )民( )有( )权(權)利( )决(決)定( )自( )己( )的( )未( )来(來)。
    - Rénmín yǒu quánlì juédìng zìjǐ de wèilái.
    - The people have the right to decide their own future.
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 人民 → 人
- **definition:** person; human; individual; people
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts side view of a standing man, highlighting an arm and a leg ->
    - visual representation of a person
  - **interpretation:** The character 人 (rén, 'person') effectively uses simple lines to depict the essential form of a human, making it immediately recognizable and intuitive as meaning 'person.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)好( )人( )。
    - Tā shì gè hǎo rén.
    - He is a good person.
  - 每( )个(個)人( )都( )有( )责(責)任( )，为(為)社( )会(會)作( )出( )贡(貢)献(獻)。
    - Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zérèn, wèi shèhuì zuòchū gòngxiàn.
    - Every person has a responsibility to contribute to society.
  - 人在旅途中，心( )灵(靈)获(獲)得( )了( )成( )长(長)。
    - Rén zài lǚtú zhōng, xīnlíng huòdé le chéngzhǎng.
    - People gain growth along their journey.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 人](@media/glyph4eba.png)
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 人民 → 民
- **definition:** people; populace; citizenry; folk
- **pinyin:** mín
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts an eye stabbed by a needle, symbolizing oppression of the people ->
    - people
  - **interpretation:** The character 民 (mín, 'people') originally depicted an eye being pierced by a tool, a metaphor for the suffering and experiences of the common people. This imagery evolved to represent the broader concept of the populace.
- **examples:**
  - 勤( )劳(勞)的( )民( )，构(構)成( )国(國)家( )的( )支( )柱( )。
    - Qínláo de mín, gòuchéng guójiā de zhīzhù.
    - Hardworking people form the backbone of the nation.
  - 民( )是( )国(國)家( )不( )可( )忽( )视(視)的( )力( )量( )。
    - Mín shì guójiā bùkě hūshì de lìliàng.
    - The people are an indispensable force in the country.
  - 民聚沙成塔。
    - Mín jù shā chéng tǎ.
    - People can achieve great feats through unity.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 民](@media/glyph6c11.png)
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 日( )报(報)
- **traditional:** 日報
- **definition:** daily newspaper
- **pinyin:** rìbào
- **components:**
  - 日
    - rì
    - day; sun
  - 报(報)
    - bào
    - report; newspaper
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 日 (rì, "day; sun") + 報 (bào, "report; newspaper") =
    - 'daily' + 'newspaper' ->
    - 'daily newspaper'
  - **interpretation:** 日 (rì, "day; sun") signifies the regular cycle of a day. In combination with 報 (bào, "report; newspaper"), which involves providing information, it denotes a publication issued every day for delivering news and reports.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )每( )天( )都( )阅(閱)读(讀)日( )报(報)。
    - Wǒ měitiān dōu yuèdú rìbào.
    - I read the daily newspaper every day.
  - 这(這)家( )商( )店( )在( )出( )售( )各( )种(種)日(類)报(報)。
    - Zhè jiā shāngdiàn zài chūshòu gè zhǒng rìbào.
    - This store sells various daily newspapers.
  - 他( )发(發)表( )了( )一( )篇( )关(關)于(於)环(環)保( )的( )文( )章( )在( )日( )报(報)上( )。
    - Tā fābiǎo le yī piān guānyú huánbǎo de wénzhāng zài rìbào shàng.
    - He published an article about environmental protection in the daily newspaper.
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 日( )报(報) → 日
- **definition:** day; sun
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 日](@media/glyph65e5.png)
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 日( )报(報) → 报(報)
- **traditional:** 報
- **definition:** report; newspaper
- **pinyin:** bào
- **components:**
  - 㚔
    - none
    - handcuffs
  - 𠬝
    - none
    - to subdue
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 㚔 (handcuffs) + 𠬝 (to subdue) =
    - to convict; to punish; to declare guilty
  - **interpretation:** The character 報 (bào, 'report; newspaper; recompense; respond') combines the imagery of handcuffs, symbolizing restraint, and an action of subduing, suggesting punishment or declaration of a verdict. This combination reflects the idea of delivering judgment or punishment, which extends metaphorically to mean 'reporting' or 'giving an account' of something. From delivering justice comes the idea of reciprocating actions, hence the sense of 'recompense' or 'respond'.
  - **simplification:** 报 is simplified from 報 by removing the 'handcuff' component 㚔, retaining the core representing the idea of reporting and responding without the punitive nuance.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )收( )到( )了( )一( )封( )信( )，里(裡)头(頭)有( )关(關)于(於)新( )项(項)目( )的( )详(詳)细(細)报(報)告( )。
    - Tā shōudào le yī fēng xìn, lǐtou yǒu guānyú xīn xiàngmù de xiángxì bàogào.
    - He received a letter with a detailed report about the new project.
  - 我( )每( )天( )都( )看( )早( )报(報)，了( )解( )世( )界( )发(發)生( )了( )什( )么(麼)。
    - Wǒ měitiān dū kàn zǎobào, liǎojiě shìjiè fāshēngle shénme.
    - I read the morning newspaper every day to understand what's happening in the world.
  - 他( )希( )望( )用( )自( )己( )的( )努( )力( )来(來)报(報)答( )父( )母( )的( )养(養)育( )之( )恩( )。
    - Tā xīwàng yòng zìjǐ de nǔlì lái bàodá fùmǔ de yǎngyù zhī ēn.
    - He hopes to use his own efforts to recompense his parents for their upbringing.
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 日( )报(報) → 报(報) → 㚔
- **definition:** handcuffs
- **pinyin:** none
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts handcuffs; originally a pictorial representation of shackles or restraints
  - **interpretation:** The character 㚔 is a pictogram that visually represents handcuffs. It does not rely on phonetic or semantic combinations with other components, instead directly depicting the shape and concept of handcuffs for its meaning. This iconography is consistent with its definition as a tool of restraint.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 㚔](@media/glyph3694.png)
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 日( )报(報) → 报(報) → 𠬝
- **definition:** to subdue
- **pinyin:** none
- **components:**
  - 凡
    - fán
    - tray
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 𠬝 (original pictogram for serving) ->
    - 凡 (fán, 'tray') added as semantic + 𠬝 (fú, 'to subdue') as phonetic ->
    - 服
  - **interpretation:** 𠬝 originally depicted the concept of serving or subduing. When 凡 (fán, 'tray') was added, it contributed to the meaning of serving or submitting, while 𠬝 provided the phonetic component. This later evolved into the character 服.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 𠬝](@media/glyph20b1d.png)
### 人( )民( )日( )报(報) → 日( )报(報) → 报(報) → 𠬝 → 凡
- **definition:** tray
- **pinyin:** fán
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a plate or flat dish ->
    - borrowed for abstract meanings ->
    - unrelated to 几(幾) (jī, 'small table')
  - **interpretation:** 凡 (fán, 'ordinary') originally depicted a flat dish or plate. Over time, it expanded to encompass broader abstract meanings and is unrelated to the character 几(幾) which represents a small table.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)平( )凡( )的( )人( )。
    - tā shì gè píngfán de rén.
    - He is an ordinary person.
  - 凡( )人( )都( )可( )能( )犯( )错(錯)，从(從)错(錯)误(誤)中( )成( )长(長)。
    - fán rén dōu kěnéng fàncuò, cóng cuòwù zhōng chéngzhǎng.
    - Ordinary people may make mistakes, and grow from them.
  - 他凡去旅行，总(總)能( )发(發)现(現)新( )的( )风(風)景( )。
    - tā fán qù lǚxíng, zǒng néng fāxiàn xīn de fēngjǐng.
    - Whenever he travels, he always discovers new sceneries.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 凡](@media/glyph51e1.png)

## 日本

## Japan
### Rìběn
---
- **definition:** Japan
- **pinyin:** Rìběn
- **components:**
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun; day; date
  - 本
    - běn
    - origin; root; source; foundation
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 日 (rì, "sun") and 本 (běn, "origin") together signify Japan as the "origin of the sun." This reflects the geographical position of Japan east of the Asian continent, where the sun rises.
  - **interpretation:** Japan is called 日本 because it is located to the east of the Asian mainland, where the sun rises, symbolizing the "origin of the sun."
- **examples:**
  - 我( )今( )年( )计(計)划(劃)去( )日( )本( )，体(體)验(驗)当(當)地( )文( )化( )。
    - Wǒ jīnnián jìhuà qù Rìběn, tǐyàn dāngdì wénhuà.
    - I plan to go to Japan this year, to experience the local culture.
  - 日( )本( )美( )食( )吸( )引( )众(眾)多( )游(遊)客( )。
    - Rìběn měishí xīyǐn zhòngduō yóukè.
    - Japanese cuisine attracts many tourists.
### 日本 → 日
- **definition:** sun; day; date
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 日](@media/glyph65e5.png)
### 日本 → 本
- **definition:** origin; root; source; foundation
- **pinyin:** běn
- **components:**
  - 木
    - mù
    - tree
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 木 (mù, "tree") with horizontal stroke at root ->
    - emphasizes the root of the tree ->
    - "root"
  - **interpretation:** The character 本(běn, "root") is formed by taking 木 (mù, "tree") and highlighting its base with an extra horizontal stroke, symbolizing the root. This visualization directly leads to the meaning 'root,' as the base of a tree is its foundation and source of growth.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)本( )书(書)很( )有( )趣( )。
    - Zhè běn shū hěn yǒuqù.
    - This book is very interesting.
  - 树(樹)的( )本( )质(質)是( )坚(堅)固( )的( )。
    - Shù de běnzhì shì jiāngù de.
    - The essence of the tree is strong.
  - 从(從)本( )地( )出( )发(發)，我( )们(們)很( )快( )就( )能( )到( )达(達)那( )儿(兒)。
    - Cóng běndì chūfā, wǒmen hěn kuài jiù néng dàodá nà'er.
    - Starting from the local area, we can reach there quickly.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 本](@media/glyph672c.png)
### 日本 → 本 → 木
- **definition:** tree
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tree with branches and roots
  - **interpretation:** The character 木(mù) depicts a tree, showing the trunk in the center and two slanting lines representing branches or roots. It directly signifies the concept of wood or a tree.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)把( )椅( )子( )的( )主( )要( )材( )料( )是( )木( )。
    - Zhè bǎ yǐzi de zhǔyào cáiliào shì mù.
    - The main material of this chair is wood.
  - 工匠精心雕刻木，制成精美家具。
    - Gōngjiàng jīngxīn diāo kè mù, zhìchéng jīngměi jiājù.
    - The craftsman carefully carved the wood, crafting exquisite furniture.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 木](@media/glyph6728.png)

  - 民

- **etymology:**

  - **description:** 人 (rén, "person") denotes an individual or human being. 民 (mín, "people") signifies the people or populace. Together, 人民 (rénmín, "the people") represents the collective body of individuals within a community or nation.
  - **interpretation:** 人 (rén, "person") signifies an individual human, while 民 (mín, "people") refers to the collective populace. The combination of these characters captures the concept of all individuals forming a nation or society. The image of many 人 (rén, "person") constituting a single 民 (mín, "people") group effectively conveys the idea of a community or population as a whole.
- **examples:**
  - 人民是( )国(國)家( )的( )基( )础(礎)。
    - Rénmín shì guójiā de jīchǔ.
    - The people are the foundation of the nation.
  - 政( )府( )应(應)该(該)服( )务(務)人( )民( )，不是官僚。
    - Zhèngfǔ yīnggāi fúwù rénmín, bùshì guānliáo.
    - The government should serve the people, not bureaucrats.
  - 人( )民( )有( )权(權)利( )决(決)定( )自( )己( )的( )未( )来(來)。
    - Rénmín yǒu quánlì juédìng zìjǐ de wèilái.
    - The people have the right to decide their own future.
### 人民 → 人
- **definition:** person; human; individual; people
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts side view of a standing man, highlighting an arm and a leg ->
    - visual representation of a person
  - **interpretation:** The character 人 (rén, 'person') effectively uses simple lines to depict the essential form of a human, making it immediately recognizable and intuitive as meaning 'person.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)好( )人( )。
    - Tā shì gè hǎo rén.
    - He is a good person.
  - 每( )个(個)人( )都( )有( )责(責)任( )，为(為)社( )会(會)作( )出( )贡(貢)献(獻)。
    - Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zérèn, wèi shèhuì zuòchū gòngxiàn.
    - Every person has a responsibility to contribute to society.
  - 人在旅途中，心( )灵(靈)获(獲)得( )了( )成( )长(長)。
    - Rén zài lǚtú zhōng, xīnlíng huòdé le chéngzhǎng.
    - People gain growth along their journey.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 人](@media/glyph4eba.png)
### 人民 → 民
- **definition:** people; populace; citizenry; folk
- **pinyin:** mín
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts an eye stabbed by a needle, symbolizing oppression of the people ->
    - people
  - **interpretation:** The character 民 (mín, 'people') originally depicted an eye being pierced by a tool, a metaphor for the suffering and experiences of the common people. This imagery evolved to represent the broader concept of the populace.
- **examples:**
  - 勤( )劳(勞)的( )民( )，构(構)成( )国(國)家( )的( )支( )柱( )。
    - Qínláo de mín, gòuchéng guójiā de zhīzhù.
    - Hardworking people form the backbone of the nation.
  - 民( )是( )国(國)家( )不( )可( )忽( )视(視)的( )力( )量( )。
    - Mín shì guójiā bùkě hūshì de lìliàng.
    - The people are an indispensable force in the country.
  - 民聚沙成塔。
    - Mín jù shā chéng tǎ.
    - People can achieve great feats through unity.
- **historical forms:**

## thermo-sensitive
### rèmǐn
---
- **traditional:** 熱敏
- **definition:** thermo-sensitive
- **pinyin:** rèmǐn
- **components:**
  - 热(熱)
    - rè
    - heat; hot
  - 敏
    - mǐn
    - sensitive; quick
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 熱 (rè, "heat; hot") refers to high temperature or warmth. 敏 (mǐn, "sensitive; quick") denotes being perceptive or responsive, especially to external stimuli. Together, these characters form a term for something that is sensitive or responsive to heat, specifically 'thermo-sensitive' in English.
  - **interpretation:** 熱 (rè, "heat; hot") implies the application of temperature or warmth. 敏 (mǐn, "sensitive; quick") suggests an ability or readiness to respond. Combined, 熱敏 (rèmǐn, "thermo-sensitive") describes a condition or material that reacts or changes in response to heat, capturing the idea of sensitivity through the presence of warmth.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)种(種)纸(紙)是( )热(熱)敏( )纸(紙)。
    - Zhè zhǒng zhǐ shì rèmǐn zhǐ.
    - This type of paper is thermo-sensitive paper.
  - 热(熱)敏( )反( )应(應)器( )在( )高( )温(溫)下( )启(啟)动(動)。
    - Rèmǐn fǎnyìngqì zài gāowēn xià qǐdòng.
    - The thermo-sensitive reactor activates at high temperatures.
  - 他( )们(們)开(開)发(發)了( )一( )种(種)热(熱)敏( )材( )料( )，用( )于(於)温(溫)度( )监(監)测(測)。
    - Tāmen kāifā le yī zhǒng rèmǐn cáiliào, yòng yú wēndù jiāncè.
    - They developed a thermo-sensitive material for temperature monitoring.
### 热(熱)敏( ) → 热(熱)
- **traditional:** 熱
- **definition:** heat; hot
- **pinyin:** rè
- **components:**
  - 埶
    - yì
    - art
  - 灬
    - huǒ
    - fire radical
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 埶 (yì, "art") + 灬 (fire radical) =
    - artfully controlled fire ->
    - heat ->
    - semiotic phonetic 执(埶)
  - **interpretation:** The character 熱 (rè) in its traditional form combines 埶 (yì, "art") which phonetically hints at the pronunciation and 灬 indicating fire, to designate an artfully controlled fire representing heat. The fire radical emphasizes the concept of heat and heat generation.
  - **simplification:** Simplified by replacing 埶 with 执(埶) (zhí, "to hold") for phonetic reduction and easier writing.
- **examples:**
  - 今( )天( )很( )热(熱)。
    - Jīntiān hěn rè.
    - It is very hot today.
  - 他( )发(發)热(熱)，需( )要( )去( )医(醫)院( )。
    - Tā fā rè, xūyào qù yīyuàn.
    - He has a fever and needs to go to the hospital.
  - 夏( )天( )特( )别(別)热(熱)，游泳是消暑之良方。
    - Xiàtiān tèbié rè, yóuyǒng shì xiāoshǔ zhī liángfāng.
    - Summer is especially hot, and swimming is an excellent way to cool off.
### 热(熱)敏( ) → 热(熱) → 埶
- **definition:** art
- **pinyin:** yì
- **components:**
  - 木
    - mù
    - tree
  - 丮
    - jī
    - to hold
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 木(木, "tree") + 丮(丮, "to hold") ->
    - kneeling person planting a sapling ->
    - 坴(圥, soil) for 土, 木 corrupted to 坴, 丮 changed from 丸 ->
    - 埶
  - **interpretation:** 埶 represents the concept of planting, showing a person holding and planting a sapling into soil. This imagery is appropriate for art as it reflects careful nurturing and creation. The character serves as the original form of 藝, reinforcing its association with cultivated skills.
- **historical forms:**

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 热(熱) → 埶 → 木
- **definition:** tree
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tree with branches and roots
  - **interpretation:** The character 木(mù) depicts a tree, showing the trunk in the center and two slanting lines representing branches or roots. It directly signifies the concept of wood or a tree.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)把( )椅( )子( )的( )主( )要( )材( )料( )是( )木( )。
    - Zhè bǎ yǐzi de zhǔyào cáiliào shì mù.
    - The main material of this chair is wood.
  - 工匠精心雕刻木，制成精美家具。
    - Gōngjiàng jīngxīn diāo kè mù, zhìchéng jīngměi jiājù.
    - The craftsman carefully carved the wood, crafting exquisite furniture.

![Historical forms of 木](@media/glyph6728.png)

- **pinyin:** jī

  - **interpretation:** The character 丮(jī) is a pictogram illustrating a person in a kneeling position with their arms extended forward, symbolizing the act of holding something.

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 热(熱) → 灬
- **definition:** fire radical
- **pinyin:** huǒ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts flames or fire at the bottom of a character
  - **interpretation:** The character 灬(biāo, 'fire') visually resembles flames and is used to represent fire-related concepts, especially as a radical component in many Chinese characters.
- **examples:**
  - 灬( )是( )燃( )烧(燒)的( )象( )征(徵)。
    - biāo shì ránshāo de xiàngzhēng.
    - Fire is a symbol of burning.
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，灬( )映( )出( )温(溫)暖( )的( )光( )芒( )。
    - yè mù jiàng lín shí, biāo yìng chū wēnnuǎn de guāngmáng.
    - When night falls, fire radiates a warm glow.
  - 狂( )风(風)暴( )雨( )后(後)，灬( )象( )征(徵)重( )生( )。
    - kuáng fēng bào yǔ hòu, biāo xiàngzhēng chóngshēng.
    - After the storm, fire symbolizes rebirth.
### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏
- **definition:** sensitive; quick
- **pinyin:** mǐn
- **components:**
  - 攵(攴)
    - pū
    - tap
  - 每
    - měi
    - each
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 攵(攴) (pū, 'tap') + phonetic: 每 (měi, 'each') =
    - quick movements ->
    - sensitive; quick
  - **interpretation:** The character 敏(mǐn) suggests quick or sensitive actions, combining 攴 (pū, 'tap'), symbolizing action, with 每 (měi, 'each'), which hints at repetitive or efficient action.
- **examples:**
  - 他做事十分敏捷，总(總)能( )迅( )速( )反( )应(應)。
    - Tā zuò shì shífēn mǐnjié, zǒng néng xùnsù fǎnyìng.
    - He is very quick in his actions, always responding swiftly.
  - 她天生很敏感，容( )易( )察( )觉(覺)情( )绪(緒)变(變)化( )。
    - Tā tiānshēng hěn mǐngǎn, róngyì chájué qíngxù biànhuà.
    - She is naturally sensitive and easily perceives mood changes.

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 攵
- **definition:** tap
- **pinyin:** pū
- **components:**
  - 又
    - yòu
    - hand
  - 卜
    - bǔ
    - divination
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a hand 又 (yòu, "hand") holding a tool, stylized as 卜 (bǔ, "divination") ->
    - meaning "to hit"
  - **interpretation:** 攵 is a stylized pictogram originally depicting a hand holding a tool or weapon, representing action or hitting. The hand component evolved into a more uniform stylistic form using 卜 while preserving the core meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他埋怨我的方式总(總)是( )冲(衝)动(動)而( )不( )经(經)过(過)思( )考( )。
    - Tā mányuàn wǒ de fāngshì zǒng shì chōngdòng ér bù jīngguò sīkǎo.
    - He complains that my actions are always impulsive and thoughtless.
  - 需( )要( )的( )不( )是( )空( )谈(談)，而( )是( )切( )实(實)的( )行( )动(動)。
    - Xūyào de bù shì kōngtán, ér shì qiēshí de xíngdòng.
    - What is needed is not empty talk, but concrete action.
### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 攵 → 又
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** yòu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a right hand
  - **interpretation:** The character 又 (yòu, "hand") is a pictogram representing a right hand, showing the fingers and palm, which highlights its original meaning related to manual actions.
- **examples:**
  - 「又( )」这(這)个(個)字( )最( )早( )来(來)源( )于(於)手( )。
    - 「Yòu」 zhè gè zì zuì zǎo láiyuán yú shǒu。
    - The character '又' originally comes from the hand.
  - 很( )多( )汉(漢)字( )含( )有( )又( )部( )首( )，表( )示( )与(與)手( )有( )关(關)的( )动(動)作( )。
    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。
    - Gǔrén yòng Yòu miáohuì shǒu de xíngtài, tǐxiàn le gǔ hànzì de dútè mèilì。
    - Ancient people used '又' to depict the shape of the hand, showcasing the unique charm of ancient Chinese characters.

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 攵 → 卜
- **definition:** divination
- **pinyin:** bǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a crack in an oracle bone
  - **interpretation:** The character 卜 (bǔ, 'divination') directly depicts a crack being used in oracle bones to divine future events. This literal representation naturally extends to the concept of divination.
- **examples:**
  - 在古代，人( )们(們)常( )用( )龟(龜)甲( )进(進)行( )卜( )卦( )。

    - In ancient times, people often used turtle shells for divination.
  - 他( )对(對)未( )来(來)充( )满(滿)疑( )虑(慮)，所( )以( )决(決)定( )找( )人( )卜( )卦( )。
    - Tā duì wèilái chōngmǎn yílǜ, suǒyǐ juédìng zhǎo rén bǔguà.
    - He was full of doubts about the future, so he decided to find someone for divination.
  - 在( )占( )星( )术(術)中( )，卜( )法( )常( )被( )用( )来(來)预(預)测(測)未( )来(來)。
    - Zài zhānxīngshù zhōng, bǔfǎ cháng bèi yòng lái yùcè wèilái.

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 每
- **definition:** each

  - 屮
    - chè
    - sprout
  - 母
    - mǔ

  - **description:**
    - semantic 屮 (sprout) + phonetic 母 (mǔ, "mother") ->
    - adult woman ready for marriage and generation ->
    - each time
  - **interpretation:** The character 每 (měi, "each") combines the semantic element 屮 ("sprout") with the phonetic 母 (mǔ, "mother"). The sprout component implies growth and maturity, suggesting a woman has reached adulthood. This connects to the idea of 'each time' through the readiness for recurring life cycles or activities, such as marriage and childbearing.
- **examples:**
  - 每( )个(個)人( )都( )有( )自( )己( )的( )梦(夢)想( )。

    - Tā měitiān zǎoshang duànliàn shēntǐ.
    - He exercises every morning.

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 每 → 屮
- **definition:** sprout
- **pinyin:** chè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a sprout emerging from the ground
  - **interpretation:** 屮 (chè, 'sprout') is a pictogram that directly represents a young plant or sprout emerging from the ground. Its visual form captures the essence of new growth, and it historically served as an early representation of plant life or new vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 春( )雨( )滋( )润(潤)后(後)，小( )屮( )冲(沖)破( )泥( )土( )而( )出( )。

### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 每 → 母
- **definition:** mother
- **pinyin:** mǔ
- **components:**
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - woman
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** differentiated form
  - **description:**
    - 女 (nǚ, "woman") + addition of two dots ->
    - distinguished as 母 (mǔ, "mother")
  - **interpretation:** The character 母 (mǔ, "mother") is a differentiated form of 女 (nǚ, "woman") with the addition of two dots to distinguish it. In early script forms, both 母 (mǔ, "mother") and 女 (nǚ, "woman") were represented by the same form as 女, necessitating the addition of dots to create a visual distinction. The character 毋, originally borrowed from 母, further specialized by changing the dots to a stroke to avoid confusion between the characters.
- **examples:**
  - 母( )给(給)了( )我( )无(無)限( )的( )温(溫)暖( )。
    - mǔ gěi le wǒ wúxiàn de wēnnuǎn
    - Mother has given me endless warmth.
  - 我( )的( )母( )温(溫)柔( )而( )坚(堅)毅( )，培( )养(養)了( )我( )良( )好( )的( )品( )格( )。
    - wǒ de mǔ wēnróu ér jiānyì, péiyǎng le wǒ liánghǎo de pǐngé
    - My mother is gentle and strong, nurturing my good character.
- **historical forms:**

![Historical forms of 母](@media/glyph6bcd.png)
### 热(熱)敏( ) → 敏 → 每 → 母 → 女
- **definition:** woman
- **pinyin:** nǚ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a woman kneeling or standing ->
    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )才( )华(華)横(橫)溢( )的( )女( )，赢(贏)得( )了( )全( )场(場)注( )目( )。
    - Nà wèi cáihuá héngyì de nǚ, yíng dé le quánchǎng zhùmù.
    - That talented woman won everyone's attention.
  - 古( )时(時)候( )‘女( )’常( )被( )用( )来(來)象( )征(徵)温(溫)柔( )，而( )如( )今( )它( )代( )表( )独(獨)立( )自( )信( )。
    - Gǔshíhòu ‘nǚ’ cháng bèi yòng lái xiàngzhēng wēnróu, ér rújīn tā dàibiǎo dúlì zìxìn.
    - In ancient times, the character '女' was often used to symbolize gentleness, whereas today it represents independence and confidence.

## 拿

## take; hold
### ná
---
- **definition:** take; hold
- **pinyin:** ná
- **components:**
  - 奴
    - nú
    - slave
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - phonetic 奴 (nú, "slave") + semantic 手 (shǒu, "hand") ->
    - take with the hand
  - **interpretation:** 奴 (nú, "slave") provides the phonetic element, while 手 (shǒu, "hand") suggests the action involves manual effort, leading to the meaning "take" or "hold."
- **examples:**
  - 请(請)把( )这(這)个(個)包( )拿( )给(給)我( )。
    - Qǐng bǎ zhège bāo ná gěi wǒ.
    - Please take this bag to me.
  - 他( )必( )须(須)拿( )出( )证(證)明( )文( )件( )，才( )能( )进(進)入( )大( )楼(樓)。
    - Tā bìxū ná chū zhèngmíng wénjiàn, cái néng jìnrù dàlóu.
    - He has to take out the proof documents to enter the building.
  - 请(請)先( )拿( )号(號)，然( )后(後)在( )座( )位( )上( )等( )候( )。
    - Qǐng xiān ná hào, ránhòu zài zuòwèi shàng děnghòu.
    - Please take a number first, then wait at the seat.
- **historical forms:**
